<type>file</type>
<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
4: # This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
5: #
6: # Usage:
7: #   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
8: #   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
9: #   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
10: #   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
11: #   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
12: #   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
13: #   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
14: #
15: # Note: By default, runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions for autonomous operation.
16: #       Use --interactive flag if you want to approve each file operation.
17: 
18: set -e
19: 
20: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
21: AUTOMATION_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
22: PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$AUTOMATION_DIR")"
23: PRD_FILE="${PRD_FILE:-$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json}"
24: LOG_DIR="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs"
25: PROGRESS_LOG="$LOG_DIR/progress.txt"
26: AGENTS_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/AGENTS.md"
27: CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR="$LOG_DIR/claude_outputs"
28: AGENT_PROFILES_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
29: MONITOR_STATUS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/agent-status.json"
30: 
31: # Worktree configuration (set by --worktree flag)
32: WORKTREE_MODE=""        # "primary", "independent", or "" for main
33: MAIN_PRD_FILE=""        # Points to main repo's prd.json when in worktree mode
34: REVIEW_PHASE=""         # Phase name for --review-phase mode
35: CURRENT_PHASE=""        # Current phase being executed (for cost tracking)
36: 
37: # Configuration
38: CLAUDE_CMD="${CLAUDE_CMD:-claude}"  # Allow override via environment
39: MAX_RETRIES=2
40: RETRY_DELAY=5
41: # Orchestration mode — inherited from run-agent-orchestration.sh or set directly
42: ORCH_MODE="${ORCH_MODE:-bash}"
43: 
44: # Effort -> model + max-turns mapping
45: # Stories carry an optional "effort" field: low | medium (default) | high
46: # These map to a model and a max-turns cap for the Claude CLI invocation.
47: EFFORT_MODEL_LOW="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
48: EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
49: EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH="claude-opus-4-6"
50: 
51: # resolve_effort_settings <story_id>
52: # Sets STORY_MODEL and STORY_MAX_TURNS globals based on story's effort field.
53: resolve_effort_settings() {
54:     local story_id="$1"
55:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
56:     local effort
57:     effort=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
58:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .effort // "medium"' \
59:         "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "medium")
60: 
61:     case "$effort" in
62:         low)
63:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_LOW"
64:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=10
65:             ;;
66:         high)
67:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH"
68:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=""
69:             ;;
70:         *)  # medium (default)
71:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM"
72:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=30
73:             ;;
74:     esac
75:     log "  Effort[$effort] -> model=$(basename $STORY_MODEL) turns=${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-unlimited}"
76: }
77: 
78: # resolve_provider_settings <story_id>
79: # Reads aiProvider from the story and sets STORY_PROVIDER global.
80: # Values: claude-sonnet | claude-opus | opencode | codex | epam (default: claude-sonnet)
81: resolve_provider_settings() {
82:     local story_id="$1"
83:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
84:     STORY_PROVIDER=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
85:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .aiProvider // "claude-sonnet"' \
86:         "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "claude-sonnet")
87:     log "  Provider[$STORY_PROVIDER] -> CLI=$(provider_to_cli "$STORY_PROVIDER")"
88: }
89: 
90: # provider_to_cli <provider>
91: # Returns the CLI binary name for a given aiProvider value.
92: provider_to_cli() {
93:     case "$1" in
94:         opencode)       echo "opencode" ;;
95:         codex)          echo "codex" ;;
96:         codemie-claude) echo "codemie-claude" ;;
97:         epam)           echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # epam: treat same as claude for now
98:         *)              echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # claude-sonnet, claude-opus, etc.
99:     esac
100: }
101: 
102: # normalize_provider_json <provider> <raw_jsonl_file> <out_json_file>
103: # Converts provider-specific JSONL output into a normalized JSON object
104: # matching Claude's format: {result, total_cost_usd, usage.{input_tokens,output_tokens}}
105: normalize_provider_json() {
106:     local provider="$1"
107:     local raw_file="$2"
108:     local out_file="$3"
109:     case "$provider" in
110:         opencode)
111:             # OpenCode emits JSONL stream; try step_finish first, then fall back to any cost/usage field
112:             local sf_line
113:             sf_line=$(grep '"type":"step_finish"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
114:             if [ -z "$sf_line" ]; then
115:                 sf_line=$(grep -E '"cost"|"total_cost"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo '{}')
116:             fi
117:             sf_line="${sf_line:-{\}}"
118:             # Extract text parts for result summary
119:             local result_text
120:             result_text=$(grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
121:                 | jq -rs '[.[].part.text // .[].text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "opencode run completed")
122:             jq -n \
123:                 --arg rt "$result_text" \
124:                 --argjson sf "$sf_line" \
125:                 '{result: $rt,
126:                   total_cost_usd: ($sf.cost // $sf.part.cost // $sf.total_cost // 0),
127:                   usage: {
128:                       input_tokens:  ($sf.tokens.input  // $sf.part.tokens.input  // $sf.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
129:                       output_tokens: ($sf.tokens.output // $sf.part.tokens.output // $sf.usage.output_tokens // 0)
130:                   }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
131:             ;;
132:         codex)
133:             # Codex emits JSONL stream; turn.completed has usage (no cost field)
134:             local tc_line
135:             tc_line=$(grep '"type":"turn.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
136:             tc_line="${tc_line:-{\}}"
137:             local result_text
138:             result_text=$(grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
139:                 | jq -rs '[.[].item.text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "codex exec completed")
140:             jq -n \
141:                 --arg rt "$result_text" \
142:                 --argjson tc "$tc_line" \
143:                 '{result: $rt,
144:                   total_cost_usd: 0,
145:                   usage: {
146:                       input_tokens:  ($tc.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
147:                       output_tokens: ($tc.usage.output_tokens // 0)
148:                   }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
149:             ;;
150:         codemie-claude)
151:             # codemie-claude: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
152:             ;;
153:         epam)
154:             # epam: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
155:             ;;
156:         *)
157:             # Claude: already emits normalized JSON; nothing to do
158:             ;;
159:     esac
160: }
161: 
162: # Claude CLI permission flags
163: # These allow Claude to read/write files and execute commands without prompting
164: CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
165:     "--dangerously-skip-permissions"  # Skip all permission prompts for autonomous operation
166: )
167: 
168: # Alternative: Use granular permissions (uncomment if preferred over skip-permissions)
169: # CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
170: #     "--allowedTools" "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash"
171: # )
172: 
173: # Colors for output
174: RED='\033[0;31m'
175: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
176: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
177: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
178: CYAN='\033[0;36m'
179: MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
180: WHITE='\033[1;37m'
181: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
182: 
183: # Logging functions
184: log() {
185:     echo -e "${BLUE}[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')]${NC} $1"
186:     echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
187: }
188: 
189: error() {
190:     echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1" >&2
191:     echo "[ERROR] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
192: }
193: 
194: success() {
195:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
196:     echo "[SUCCESS] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
197: }
198: 
199: warning() {
200:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
201:     echo "[WARNING] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
202: }
203: 
204: info() {
205:     echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
206: }
207: 
208: # Initialize directories and logs
209: initialize() {
210:     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$PROGRESS_LOG")"
211:     mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR"
212: 
213:     if [ ! -f "$PROGRESS_LOG" ]; then
214:         cat > "$PROGRESS_LOG" << EOF
215: === EPAM CLI Orchestration Progress Log ===
216: Started: $(date)
217: Project: $(jq -r '.project.name // "Unknown"' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
218: ==========================================
219: 
220: EOF
221:     fi
222: }
223: 
224: # Check prerequisites
225: check_prerequisites() {
226:     # Check for jq
227:     if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
228:         error "jq is required but not installed. Install with: sudo apt install jq"
229:         exit 1
230:     fi
231: 
232:     # Check for Claude CLI
233:     if ! command -v "$CLAUDE_CMD" &> /dev/null; then
234:         error "Claude CLI not found. Expected command: $CLAUDE_CMD"
235:         error "Install Claude Code CLI or set CLAUDE_CMD environment variable"
236:         exit 1
237:     fi
238: 
239:     # Check PRD file
240:     if [ ! -f "$PRD_FILE" ]; then
241:         error "PRD file not found at $PRD_FILE"
242:         exit 1
243:     fi
244: 
245:     # Validate PRD JSON
246:     if ! jq empty "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
247:         error "PRD file is not valid JSON"
248:         exit 1
249:     fi
250: 
251:     success "Prerequisites check passed"
252: }
253: 
254: # Get story details from PRD
255: get_story_details() {
256:     local story_id=$1
257:     jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id)' "$PRD_FILE"
258: }
259: 
260: # Get story title
261: get_story_title() {
262:     local story_id=$1
263:     jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE"
264: }
265: 
266: # Get story priority (high=1, medium=2, low=3)
267: get_story_priority() {
268:     local story_id=$1
269:     local priority=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .priority // "medium"' "$PRD_FILE")
270:     case $priority in
271:         high) echo 1 ;;
272:         medium) echo 2 ;;
273:         low) echo 3 ;;
274:         *) echo 2 ;;
275:     esac
276: }
277: 
278: # Check if story exists
279: story_exists() {
280:     local story_id=$1
281:     local exists=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
282:     [ -n "$exists" ]
283: }
284: 
285: # Check if story is completed
286: is_story_completed() {
287:     local story_id=$1
288:     local completed=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .completed' "$PRD_FILE")
289:     [ "$completed" = "true" ]
290: }
291: 
292: # Get story dependencies
293: get_story_dependencies() {
294:     local story_id=$1
295:     jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .dependencies // [] | .[]' "$PRD_FILE"
296: }
297: 
298: # Check if all dependencies are satisfied (completed)
299: are_dependencies_satisfied() {
300:     local story_id=$1
301:     local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id")
302: 
303:     if [ -z "$deps" ]; then
304:         return 0  # No dependencies
305:     fi
306: 
307:     while IFS= read -r dep; do
308:         if [ -n "$dep" ] && ! is_story_completed "$dep"; then
309:             return 1  # Dependency not satisfied
310:         fi
311:     done <<< "$deps"
312: 
313:     return 0  # All dependencies satisfied
314: }
315: 
316: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
317: # check_plan_mode_required <story_id>
318: # Returns 0 (true) when the story's complexity triggers plan mode.
319: # Triggers: estimatedHours >= 6, OR deps >= 2, OR planModeRequired flag.
320: # Bypass: SKIP_PLAN_MODE=true env var.
321: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
322: check_plan_mode_required() {
323:     local story_id="$1"
324:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
325: 
326:     [ "${SKIP_PLAN_MODE:-false}" = "true" ] && return 1
327: 
328:     local estimated_hours dep_count plan_flag
329:     estimated_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
330:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
331:     dep_count=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
332:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | (.dependencies // []) | length' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
333:     plan_flag=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
334:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .technicalNotes.planModeRequired // false' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo false)
335: 
336:     [ "$plan_flag" = "true" ] && return 0
337:     # Use awk for float comparison (avoids bc dependency for this check)
338:     awk -v h="$estimated_hours" 'BEGIN{exit !(h >= 6)}' && return 0
339:     [ "${dep_count:-0}" -ge 2 ] && return 0
340:     return 1
341: }
342: 
343: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
344: # run_plan_mode <story_id>
345: # Invokes Claude in planning mode to produce execution-ready artifacts.
346: # Posts a plan_summary message to agent-messages.jsonl when complete.
347: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
348: run_plan_mode() {
349:     local story_id="$1"
350:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
351:     local plan_log="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_plan_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
352:     local plan_json="${plan_log%.log}_result.json"
353:     local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"
354: 
355:     local agent_role
356:     agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
357:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
358: 
359:     local plan_prompt
360:     plan_prompt=$(cat << PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
361: You are a planning agent. Produce an execution-ready plan for story ${story_id} BEFORE implementation begins.
362: 
363: ## Required Outputs
364: 1. Implementation steps with target file paths
365: 2. Dependency validation (each dep: satisfied yes/no, reason)
366: 3. Risk register (top 3 risks + mitigations)
367: 4. Test plan (new tests required + regression scope)
368: 5. Acceptance criteria mapping (each criterion -> implementation step)
369: 6. Cost/effort forecast (confirm or adjust estimatedHours)
370: 
371: ## On Completion
372: Append a single-line JSON record to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl:
373: {
374:   "id":"plan_${story_id}_\$(date +%s)",
375:   "timestamp":"\$(date -Iseconds)",
376:   "from_agent":"plan-agent",
377:   "to_agent":"${agent_role}",
378:   "story_id":"${story_id}",
379:   "phase_id":"${CURRENT_PHASE:-unknown}",
380:   "message_type":"plan_summary",
381:   "priority":"normal",
382:   "subject":"Plan ready for ${story_id}",
383:   "body":"<one-sentence summary of key risks/steps>",
384:   "status":"new"
385: }
386: Write it atomically: (flock -w 10 9 >> orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl; printf '%s\n' '<json>' >&9) 9>>orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl
387: 
388: ## Story to Plan
389: Read orchestrations/prd.json for story ${story_id} full details, then produce the plan above.
390: PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
391:     )
392: 
393:     log "Plan mode: generating execution plan for $story_id..."
394:     touch "$messages_jsonl"
395:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
396:     if echo "$plan_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
397:             "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" 2>/dev/null > "$plan_json"; then
398:         jq -r '.result // empty' "$plan_json" 2>/dev/null >> "$plan_log" || true
399:         success "Plan mode completed for $story_id — see $plan_log"
400:     else
401:         warning "Plan mode failed for $story_id — continuing with direct implementation"
402:     fi
403: }
404: 
405: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
406: # post_completion_message <story_id> <status>
407: # Appends a status message to agent-messages.jsonl after each story run.
408: # Only writes when ORCH_MODE=hybrid OR the bus file already exists.
409: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
410: post_completion_message() {
411:     local story_id="$1"
412:     local status="$2"   # "completed" | "failed"
413:     local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"
414:     local lock_file="${messages_jsonl}.lock"
415: 
416:     # Skip if bash-only mode and bus hasn't been seeded yet
417:     if [ "${ORCH_MODE:-bash}" != "hybrid" ] && [ ! -f "$messages_jsonl" ]; then
418:         return 0
419:     fi
420: 
421:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
422:     local agent_role
423:     agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
424:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
425: 
426:     local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-unknown}"
427:     local timestamp
428:     timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
429:     local epoch
430:     epoch=$(date +%s)
431:     local msg_id="status_${story_id}_${epoch}"
432: 
433:     local message
434:     message=$(jq -cn \
435:         --arg id      "$msg_id" \
436:         --arg ts      "$timestamp" \
437:         --arg from    "$agent_role" \
438:         --arg to      "orchestrator" \
439:         --arg sid     "$story_id" \
440:         --arg pid     "$phase_id" \
441:         --arg subj    "Story $story_id $status" \
442:         --arg body    "Story $story_id finished with status: $status" \
443:         --arg sstatus "$status" \
444:         '{
445:             id: $id, timestamp: $ts,
446:             from_agent: $from, to_agent: $to,
447:             story_id: $sid, phase_id: $pid,
448:             message_type: "status",
449:             priority: "normal",
450:             subject: $subj, body: $body,
451:             story_status: $sstatus,
452:             status: "new"
453:         }')
454: 
455:     touch "$messages_jsonl"
456:     (
457:         flock -w 10 200 || return 0
458:         echo "$message" | jq -c '.' >> "$messages_jsonl"
459:     ) 200>"$lock_file"
460: }
461: 
462: # Log event to agent-status.json if running in orchestration mode
463: log_to_monitor() {
464:     local event_type=$1
465:     local story_id=$2
466:     local message=$3
467:     local monitor_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-status.json"
468: 
469:     # Only log if monitor file exists (orchestration mode)
470:     if [ ! -f "$monitor_file" ]; then
471:         return 0
472:     fi
473: 
474:     local lane="${WORKTREE_MODE:-main}"
475:     local role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // ""' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
476:     local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
477: 
478:     # Use flock to prevent race conditions
479:     (
480:         flock -w 5 200 || return 1
481: 
482:         # Add event to events array
483:         local tmp_file="${monitor_file}.tmp.$$"
484:         jq --arg type "$event_type" \
485:            --arg story "$story_id" \
486:            --arg lane "$lane" \
487:            --arg role "$role" \
488:            --arg msg "$message" \
489:            --arg ts "$timestamp" \
490:            '.events += [{"type": $type, "story": $story, "lane": $lane, "role": $role, "message": $msg, "timestamp": $ts}]' \
491:            "$monitor_file" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$monitor_file"
492:     ) 200>"${monitor_file}.lock"
493: }
494: 
495: # Get list of available phases
496: get_phases() {
497:     jq -r '.implementationOrder | keys[]' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null
498: }
499: 
500: # Get stories for a specific phase
501: get_phase_stories() {
502:     local phase=$1
503:     jq -r --arg phase "$phase" '.implementationOrder[$phase] // [] | .[]' "$PRD_FILE"
504: }
505: 
506: # Get the phase a story belongs to
507: get_story_phase() {
508:     local story_id=$1
509:     jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.implementationOrder | to_entries[] | select(.value | contains([$id])) | .key' "$PRD_FILE" | head -1
510: }
511: 
512: # Get list of incomplete stories
513: get_incomplete_stories() {
514:     jq -r '.stories[] | select(.completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE"
515: }
516: 
517: # Get prioritized list of incomplete stories (respects phases, dependencies, priority)
518: get_prioritized_stories() {
519:     local result=()
520: 
521:     # Get phases in order
522:     local phases=$(get_phases)
523: 
524:     if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
525:         # No phases defined, fall back to all incomplete stories sorted by priority
526:         jq -r '.stories[] | select(.completed == false) | "\(.priority // "medium")|\(.id)"' "$PRD_FILE" | \
527:             sort -t'|' -k1,1 | cut -d'|' -f2
528:         return
529:     fi
530: 
531:     # Process each phase in order
532:     while IFS= read -r phase; do
533:         [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
534: 
535:         # Get stories in this phase
536:         local phase_stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
537: 
538:         # For each story in the phase, check if it's incomplete and dependencies are met
539:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
540:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
541: 
542:             if ! is_story_completed "$story_id" && are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
543:                 echo "$story_id"
544:             fi
545:         done <<< "$phase_stories"
546:     done <<< "$phases"
547: }
548: 
549: # Get next story to implement (first incomplete with satisfied dependencies)
550: get_next_story() {
551:     get_prioritized_stories | head -1
552: }
553: 
554: # List available phases with status
555: list_phases() {
556:     echo ""
557:     echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== Implementation Phases ===${NC}"
558:     echo ""
559: 
560:     local phases=$(get_phases)
561: 
562:     if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
563:         echo -e "${YELLOW}No phases defined in implementationOrder${NC}"
564:         return
565:     fi
566: 
567:     while IFS= read -r phase; do
568:         [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
569: 
570:         local total=0
571:         local completed=0
572:         local stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
573: 
574:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
575:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
576:             total=$((total + 1))
577:             if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
578:                 completed=$((completed + 1))
579:             fi
580:         done <<< "$stories"
581: 
582:         local status_color=$YELLOW
583:         local status_icon="o"
584:         if [ $completed -eq $total ] && [ $total -gt 0 ]; then
585:             status_color=$GREEN
586:             status_icon="+"
587:         elif [ $completed -gt 0 ]; then
588:             status_color=$CYAN
589:             status_icon="~"
590:         fi
591: 
592:         echo -e "${status_color}${status_icon}${NC} ${WHITE}$phase${NC} ($completed/$total completed)"
593: 
594:         # Show stories in phase
595:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
596:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
597:             local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
598:             if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
599:                 echo -e "    ${GREEN}+${NC} $story_id: $title"
600:             else
601:                 local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
602:                 local deps_info=""
603:                 if [ -n "$deps" ]; then
604:                     if are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
605:                         deps_info=" ${CYAN}(deps: $deps)${NC}"
606:                     else
607:                         deps_info=" ${RED}(blocked by: $deps)${NC}"
608:                     fi
609:                 fi
610:                 echo -e "    ${YELLOW}o${NC} $story_id: $title$deps_info"
611:             fi
612:         done <<< "$stories"
613:         echo ""
614:     done <<< "$phases"
615: }
616: 
617: # Get project context for Claude
618: get_project_context() {
619:     local stack=$(jq -r '.project.stack | to_entries | map("\(.key): \(.value)") | join(", ")' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
620:     local criteria=$(jq -r '.acceptanceCriteria | join("\n- ")' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
621: 
622:     cat << EOF
623: Project: $(jq -r '.project.name' "$PRD_FILE")
624: Description: $(jq -r '.project.description' "$PRD_FILE")
625: Tech Stack: $stack
626: 
627: Global Acceptance Criteria:
628: - $criteria
629: EOF
630: }
631: 
632: # Build prompt for Claude to implement a story
633: build_implementation_prompt() {
634:     local story_id=$1
635:     local story_json=$(get_story_details "$story_id")
636: 
637:     local title=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.title')
638:     local description=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.description')
639:     local acceptance_criteria=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.acceptanceCriteria | join("\n- ")')
640:     local technical_notes=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.technicalNotes // empty')
641:     local files=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.technicalNotes.files // [] | join(", ")')
642:     local dependencies=$(echo "$story_json" | jq -r '.dependencies | join(", ")')
643: 
644:     cat << EOF
645: Implement user story $story_id: $title
646: 
647: ## Story Description
648: $description
649: 
650: ## Acceptance Criteria
651: - $acceptance_criteria
652: 
653: ## Technical Notes
654: $([ -n "$technical_notes" ] && echo "$technical_notes" | jq -r 'to_entries | map("- \(.key): \(.value)") | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null || echo "None specified")
655: 
656: ## Files to Create/Modify
657: $files
658: 
659: ## Dependencies
660: ${dependencies:-None}
661: 
662: ## Instructions
663: 1. Implement all acceptance criteria for this story
664: 2. Follow the project's existing code patterns and conventions
665: 3. Create any necessary files in the locations specified
666: 4. Ensure code compiles/runs without errors
667: 5. Do NOT create tests unless explicitly required in acceptance criteria
668: 
669: After implementation, provide a brief summary of what was created/modified.
670: EOF
671: }
672: 
673: # Update monitor lane/story status via update-monitor.sh
674: update_monitor_status() {
675:     local event="$1"   # "start" | "complete" | "fail"
676:     local story_id="$2"
677:     local message="${3:-}"
678:     local lane="${WORKTREE_MODE:-main}"
679:     local title
680:     title=$(get_story_title "$story_id" 2>/dev/null || echo "$story_id")
681:     local role
682:     role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id==$id) | .agentRole // ""' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
683:     local update_script="$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh"
684:     [ ! -x "$update_script" ] && return 0
685:     case "$event" in
686:         start)
687:             "$update_script" story_start "$story_id" "$lane" "$role" "$title" 2>/dev/null || true
688:             ;;
689:         complete)
690:             "$update_script" story_complete "$story_id" "$lane" "$title" 2>/dev/null || true
691:             ;;
692:         fail)
693:             "$update_script" story_fail "$story_id" "$lane" "$message" 2>/dev/null || true
694:             ;;
695:     esac
696: }
697: 
698: # Invoke Claude CLI to implement a story
699: implement_story() {
700:     local story_id=$1
701:     local retry_count=0
702:     local output_file="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
703:     local story_started_at=$(date -Iseconds)
704: 
705:     local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
706:     log "Implementing story: $story_id - $title"
707:     update_monitor_status "start" "$story_id"
708: 
709:     # Check dependencies first
710:     if ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
711:         local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
712:         error "Cannot implement $story_id - dependencies not satisfied: $deps"
713:         return 1
714:     fi
715: 
716:     # Plan mode check: run planning agent before implementation if complexity thresholds met
717:     if check_plan_mode_required "$story_id"; then
718:         log "Plan mode required for $story_id (estimatedHours>=6, deps>=2, or flag set)"
719:         run_plan_mode "$story_id"
720:     fi
721: 
722:     # Resolve effort -> model + max-turns for this story (stable across retries)
723:     resolve_effort_settings "$story_id"
724:     local model_flag=()
725:     local turns_flag=()
726:     [ -n "${STORY_MODEL:-}" ]     && model_flag=(--model "$STORY_MODEL")
727:     [ -n "${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-}" ] && turns_flag=(--max-turns "$STORY_MAX_TURNS")
728: 
729:     # Resolve aiProvider -> which CLI binary to use
730:     resolve_provider_settings "$story_id"
731:     local story_cli
732:     story_cli=$(provider_to_cli "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}")
733: 
734:     while [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
735:         # Rebuild prompt each attempt: retry_count and KB ID must reflect current state
736:         local next_kb_id
737:         next_kb_id=$(get_next_kb_id)
738:         local prompt
739:         prompt="$(build_implementation_prompt "$story_id")
740: $(build_kb_prompt_section "$story_id" "$retry_count" "$next_kb_id")"
741: 
742:         # Log the prompt
743:         echo "=== Prompt for $story_id (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
744:         echo "$prompt" >> "$output_file"
745:         echo "=== End Prompt ===" >> "$output_file"
746:         echo "" >> "$output_file"
747: 
748:         log "Invoking $story_cli (attempt $((retry_count + 1))/$((MAX_RETRIES + 1)))..."
749: 
750:         # Change to project root for the CLI to have correct context
751:         cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
752: 
753:         echo "=== $story_cli Output (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
754: 
755:         local json_result_file="${output_file%.log}_result.json"
756:         local invoke_success=false
757: 
758:         case "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}" in
759:             opencode)
760:                 # OpenCode: pass prompt via temp file (prompts can exceed arg limits)
761:                 # --format json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
762:                 local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
763:                 local prompt_file="${json_result_file%.json}_prompt.txt"
764:                 echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
765:                 if opencode run --format json "$(cat "$prompt_file")" \
766:                         > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
767:                     normalize_provider_json "opencode" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
768:                     # Append text output to log
769:                     grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
770:                         | jq -r '.part.text // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
771:                     invoke_success=true
772:                 fi
773:                 rm -f "$prompt_file"
774:                 ;;
775:             codex)
776:                 # Codex: reads prompt from stdin when '-' is passed
777:                 # --json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
778:                 local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
779:                 if echo "$prompt" | codex exec --json - \
780:                         > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
781:                     normalize_provider_json "codex" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
782:                     # Append text output to log
783:                     grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
784:                         | jq -r '.item.text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
785:                     invoke_success=true
786:                 fi
787:                 ;;
788:             codemie-claude)
789:                 # codemie-claude: same invocation pattern as claude — --print --output-format json
790:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE codemie-claude --print --output-format json \
791:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
792:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
793:                     invoke_success=true
794:                 fi
795:                 ;;
796:             epam)
797:                 # epam: treat same as claude — same CLI, same output format
798:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
799:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
800:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
801:                     invoke_success=true
802:                 fi
803:                 ;;
804:             *)
805:                 # Claude (claude-sonnet, claude-opus, or any claude-* value)
806:                 # --print --output-format json captures cost+tokens in a single JSON object
807:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
808:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
809:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
810:                     invoke_success=true
811:                 fi
812:                 ;;
813:         esac
814: 
815:         if [ "$invoke_success" = true ]; then
816:             # Extract human-readable result text and append to output log
817:             if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
818:                 jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
819:             fi
820:             echo "" >> "$output_file"
821:             echo "=== End $story_cli Output ===" >> "$output_file"
822:             success "$story_cli completed implementation for $story_id"
823:             update_monitor_status "complete" "$story_id" "Implementation succeeded"
824:             append_cost_record "$story_id" "completed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
825:             post_completion_message "$story_id" "completed"
826:             return 0
827:         else
828:             local exit_code=$?
829:             # Still capture any partial JSON output
830:             if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
831:                 jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
832:             fi
833:             echo "" >> "$output_file"
834:             echo "=== $story_cli exited with code $exit_code ===" >> "$output_file"
835: 
836:             retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
837:             if [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; then
838:                 warning "$story_cli failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
839:                 sleep $RETRY_DELAY
840:             fi
841:         fi
842:     done
843: 
844:     error "Failed to implement $story_id after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
845:     update_monitor_status "fail" "$story_id" "Failed after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
846:     append_cost_record "$story_id" "failed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
847:     post_completion_message "$story_id" "failed"
848:     return 1
849: }
850: 
851: # Update story status in PRD
852: update_story_status() {
853:     local story_id=$1
854:     local status=$2  # "completed" or "failed"
855:     local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
856:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
857:     local lock_file="${prd_target}.lock"
858: 
859:     local tmp_file="${prd_target}.tmp.$$"
860: 
861:     (
862:         flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $prd_target"; return 1; }
863: 
864:         if [ "$status" = "completed" ]; then
865:             jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
866:                 '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {completed: true, status: "completed", completedAt: $ts}' \
867:                 "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
868:             success "Story $story_id marked as completed"
869:             update_agents_file "$story_id" "completed"
870:         else
871:             jq --arg id "$story_id" --arg ts "$timestamp" \
872:                 '(.stories[] | select(.id == $id)) |= . + {status: "failed", lastAttempt: $ts}' \
873:                 "$prd_target" > "$tmp_file" && mv "$tmp_file" "$prd_target"
874:             warning "Story $story_id marked as failed"
875:             update_agents_file "$story_id" "failed"
876:         fi
877:         # Sync PRD_FILE to orchestrations/game-prd.json if it's a non-default PRD (for dashboard viewers)
878:         local default_prd="$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json"
879:         if [ "$prd_target" != "$default_prd" ] && [ -f "$prd_target" ]; then
880:             local sync_dest="$AUTOMATION_DIR/game-prd.json"
881:             cp "$prd_target" "$sync_dest" 2>/dev/null || true
882:         fi
883:     ) 200>"$lock_file"
884: }
885: 
886: # Append a cost/time record to phase-cost.jsonl
887: # Called after each story completes (success or failure) for phase-aware tracking
888: append_cost_record() {
889:     local story_id=$1 status=$2 started_at=$3 ended_at=$4 output_file=$5 json_result_file=${6:-}
890:     local cost_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/phase-cost.jsonl"
891:     local lock_file="${cost_file}.lock"
892: 
893:     # Read story metadata from prd.json
894:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
895:     local title=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
896:     local agent_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target")
897:     local forecast_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target")
898:     local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-}"
899:     if [ -z "$phase_id" ]; then
900:         # Look up phase from implementationOrder when not set by --phase flag
901:         phase_id=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
902:             '.implementationOrder | to_entries[] | select(.value | contains([$id])) | .key' \
903:             "$prd_target" | head -1)
904:         [ -z "$phase_id" ] && phase_id="unknown"
905:     fi
906: 
907:     # Compute elapsed minutes
908:     local start_epoch=$(date -d "$started_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
909:     local end_epoch=$(date -d "$ended_at" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
910:     local elapsed_minutes=0
911:     if [ "$start_epoch" -gt 0 ] && [ "$end_epoch" -gt 0 ]; then
912:         elapsed_minutes=$(echo "scale=2; ($end_epoch - $start_epoch) / 60" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
913:     fi
914: 
915:     # Parse cost/token/turn usage from Claude CLI JSON result (--output-format json)
916:     local tokens_in=0 tokens_out=0 cost_usd=0 task_turns=0
917:     if [ -n "$json_result_file" ] && [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
918:         cost_usd=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
919:         tokens_in=$(jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
920:         tokens_out=$(jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
921:         # Turn count: Claude CLI may report num_turns or turns
922:         task_turns=$(jq -r '.num_turns // .turns // .usage.turns // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
923:         # Also capture cache tokens if present
924:         local cache_create=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
925:         local cache_read=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_read_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
926:         # Total input includes base + cache tokens
927:         tokens_in=$(( ${tokens_in:-0} + ${cache_create:-0} + ${cache_read:-0} ))
928:     fi
929:     [ -z "$tokens_in" ] && tokens_in=0
930:     [ -z "$tokens_out" ] && tokens_out=0
931:     [ -z "$cost_usd" ] && cost_usd=0
932:     [ -z "$task_turns" ] && task_turns=0
933: 
934:     # Atomic JSONL append with flock
935:     (
936:         flock -w 10 200 || { error "Could not acquire lock on $cost_file"; return 1; }
937:         jq -cn \
938:             --arg pid "$phase_id" --arg pn "$phase_id" \
939:             --arg sid "$story_id" --arg st "$title" \
940:             --arg aid "$agent_id" --arg an "$agent_id" \
941:             --argjson fh "${forecast_hours:-0}" --argjson fc 0 \
942:             --arg sa "$started_at" --arg ea "$ended_at" \
943:             --argjson em "${elapsed_minutes:-0}" --argjson cu "$cost_usd" \
944:             --argjson ti "${tokens_in:-0}" --argjson to "${tokens_out:-0}" \
945:             --argjson tt "${task_turns:-0}" \
946:             --argjson cr "${cache_read:-0}" --argjson cc "${cache_create:-0}" \
947:             --arg s "$status" --arg n "" \
948:             '{phase_id:$pid, phase_name:$pn, story_id:$sid, story_title:$st,
949:               agent_id:$aid, agent_name:$an, forecast_hours:$fh, forecast_cost_usd:$fc,
950:               started_at:$sa, ended_at:$ea, elapsed_minutes:$em,
951:               task_cost_usd:$cu, task_tokens_in:$ti, task_tokens_out:$to,
952:               task_turns:$tt, cache_read_tokens:$cr, cache_create_tokens:$cc,
953:               status:$s, notes:$n}' >> "$cost_file"
954:     ) 200>"$lock_file"
955: }
956: 
957: # Return the next sequential KB entry ID (KB-001, KB-002, ...) by reading orchestrations/agents/KB.md
958: get_next_kb_id() {
959:     local kb_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/KB.md"
960:     if [ ! -f "$kb_file" ]; then
961:         echo "KB-001"
962:         return
963:     fi
964:     local last_num
965:     last_num=$(grep -oP '(?<=^## KB-)\d+' "$kb_file" | sort -n | tail -1)
966:     if [ -z "$last_num" ]; then
967:         echo "KB-001"
968:     else
969:         printf "KB-%03d" $((last_num + 1))
970:     fi
971: }
972: 
973: # Return KB entries from orchestrations/agents/KB.md whose AgentRole matches the current story
974: get_relevant_kb_entries() {
975:     local story_id=$1
976:     local kb_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/KB.md"
977:     [ ! -f "$kb_file" ] && return
978: 
979:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
980:     local agent_role
981:     agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
982:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // ""' "$prd_target")
983:     [ -z "$agent_role" ] && return
984: 
985:     # Extract full entry blocks whose AgentRole line contains the role
986:     awk -v role="$agent_role" '
987:         /^## KB-[0-9]/ {
988:             if (entry != "" && matched) printf "%s\n", entry
989:             entry = $0 "\n"; matched = 0; next
990:         }
991:         { entry = entry $0 "\n" }
992:         /\*\*AgentRole:\*\*/ { if (index($0, role) > 0) matched = 1 }
993:         END { if (entry != "" && matched) printf "%s\n", entry }
994:     ' "$kb_file"
995: }
996: 
997: # Build the KB section appended to every implementation prompt
998: build_kb_prompt_section() {
999:     local story_id=$1
1000:     local retry_count=${2:-0}
1001:     local next_kb_id=${3:-KB-001}
1002:     local today
1003:     today=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d')
1004: 
1005:     local kb_entries
1006:     kb_entries=$(get_relevant_kb_entries "$story_id")
1007: 
1008:     local retry_note=""
1009:     [ "$retry_count" -gt 0 ] && \
1010:         retry_note="**This is retry attempt ${retry_count}** — a previous attempt failed. You MUST write a KB entry documenting what went wrong and what you changed."
1011: 
1012:     printf '\n## Relevant Knowledge Base Entries\n'
1013:     if [ -n "$kb_entries" ]; then
1014:         printf 'The following was learned from previous story implementations and is relevant to your agent role. Apply this knowledge before writing any code:\n\n'
1015:         printf '%s\n' "$kb_entries"
1016:     else
1017:         printf 'No prior KB entries match your agent role yet.\n'
1018:     fi
1019: 
1020:     printf '\n## Knowledge Base Contribution\n'
1021:     printf 'Your assigned KB entry ID for this run: **%s**\n' "$next_kb_id"
1022:     [ -n "$retry_note" ] && printf '%s\n' "$retry_note"
1023:     printf '\nIf you discover a non-obvious pattern, gotcha, or anti-pattern during this implementation (or this is a retry), append exactly one entry to `orchestrations/agents/KB.md` using this format:\n\n'
1024:     printf '```markdown\n'
1025:     printf '## %s -- %s\n\n' "$next_kb_id" "$today"
1026:     printf '**Category:** <backend|frontend|infrastructure|testing|orchestration>\n'
1027:     printf '**AgentRole:** <your agentRole from the story>\n'
1028:     printf '**Tags:** <comma-separated tech keywords, e.g. typescript, node, cli>\n'
1029:     printf '**Trigger:** <retry|first-success>\n'
1030:     printf '**StoryRef:** %s\n\n' "$story_id"
1031:     printf '<One concise paragraph: the specific pattern, gotcha, or anti-pattern. Precise enough that a future Claude instance can apply it without re-discovering it.>\n'
1032:     printf '```\n\n'
1033:     printf 'Only write an entry if the knowledge is genuinely non-obvious. Skip trivial observations.\n'
1034: }
1035: 
1036: # Update AGENTS.md with implementation record
1037: update_agents_file() {
1038:     local story_id=$1
1039:     local status=$2
1040:     local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
1041:     local phase=$(get_story_phase "$story_id")
1042: 
1043:     if [ ! -f "$AGENTS_FILE" ]; then
1044:         mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AGENTS_FILE")"
1045:         cat > "$AGENTS_FILE" << EOF
1046: # EPAM CLI Agent Learned Patterns
1047: 
1048: This file tracks implementation history and patterns discovered during autonomous development.
1049: 
1050: ---
1051: 
1052: EOF
1053:     fi
1054: 
1055:     cat >> "$AGENTS_FILE" << EOF
1056: ## $story_id: $title
1057: - **Date**: $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
1058: - **Phase**: ${phase:-unassigned}
1059: - **Status**: $status
1060: - **Log**: logs/claude_outputs/${story_id}_*.log
1061: 
1062: EOF
1063: }
1064: 
1065: # Increment iteration counter
1066: increment_iteration() {
1067:     local current=$(jq -r '.currentIteration' "$PRD_FILE")
1068:     local next=$((current + 1))
1069:     jq ".currentIteration = $next" "$PRD_FILE" > "$PRD_FILE.tmp" && mv "$PRD_FILE.tmp" "$PRD_FILE"
1070: }
1071: 
1072: # Show PRD status with phase information
1073: show_status() {
1074:     echo ""
1075:     echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== PRD Status ===${NC}"
1076:     echo ""
1077: 
1078:     local total=$(jq '.stories | length' "$PRD_FILE")
1079:     local completed=$(jq '[.stories[] | select(.completed == true)] | length' "$PRD_FILE")
1080:     local pending=$((total - completed))
1081: 
1082:     echo -e "Project: ${CYAN}$(jq -r '.project.name' "$PRD_FILE")${NC}"
1083:     echo -e "Total Stories: $total"
1084:     echo -e "Completed: ${GREEN}$completed${NC}"
1085:     echo -e "Pending: ${YELLOW}$pending${NC}"
1086:     echo ""
1087: 
1088:     # Show next recommended story
1089:     local next=$(get_next_story)
1090:     if [ -n "$next" ]; then
1091:         echo -e "Next recommended: ${WHITE}$next${NC} - $(get_story_title "$next")"
1092:         local phase=$(get_story_phase "$next")
1093:         [ -n "$phase" ] && echo -e "                 Phase: ${CYAN}$phase${NC}"
1094:     fi
1095:     echo ""
1096: 
1097:     echo -e "${CYAN}Stories by Phase:${NC}"
1098: 
1099:     local phases=$(get_phases)
1100:     if [ -n "$phases" ]; then
1101:         while IFS= read -r phase; do
1102:             [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
1103:             echo -e "\n  ${WHITE}$phase:${NC}"
1104: 
1105:             local stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
1106:             while IFS= read -r story_id; do
1107:                 [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
1108:                 local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
1109:                 local priority=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .priority // "medium"' "$PRD_FILE")
1110:                 local priority_badge=""
1111:                 case $priority in
1112:                     high) priority_badge=" ${RED}[H]${NC}" ;;
1113:                     low) priority_badge=" ${BLUE}[L]${NC}" ;;
1114:                 esac
1115: 
1116:                 if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
1117:                     echo -e "    ${GREEN}+${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge"
1118:                 elif are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
1119:                     echo -e "    ${YELLOW}o${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge ${CYAN}(ready)${NC}"
1120:                 else
1121:                     local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
1122:                     echo -e "    ${RED}x${NC} $story_id: $title$priority_badge ${RED}(blocked: $deps)${NC}"
1123:                 fi
1124:             done <<< "$stories"
1125:         done <<< "$phases"
1126:     else
1127:         # No phases, show flat list
1128:         jq -r '.stories[] | "\(.id): \(.title) [\(if .completed then "DONE" else "PENDING" end)]"' "$PRD_FILE" | while read line; do
1129:             if [[ "$line" == *"[DONE]"* ]]; then
1130:                 echo -e "  ${GREEN}+${NC} $line"
1131:             else
1132:                 echo -e "  ${YELLOW}o${NC} $line"
1133:             fi
1134:         done
1135:     fi
1136:     echo ""
1137: }
1138: 
1139: # Dry run - show what would be implemented
1140: dry_run() {
1141:     local stories=("$@")
1142:     local phase_filter=""
1143: 
1144:     echo ""
1145:     echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== Dry Run ===${NC}"
1146:     echo ""
1147: 
1148:     if [ ${#stories[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
1149:         mapfile -t stories < <(get_prioritized_stories)
1150:     fi
1151: 
1152:     if [ ${#stories[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
1153:         echo -e "${GREEN}All stories are already completed (or blocked by dependencies)!${NC}"
1154:         return
1155:     fi
1156: 
1157:     echo "The following stories would be implemented (in order):"
1158:     echo ""
1159: 
1160:     local order=1
1161:     for story_id in "${stories[@]}"; do
1162:         if ! story_exists "$story_id"; then
1163:             echo -e "  ${RED}x${NC} $story_id - NOT FOUND"
1164:             continue
1165:         fi
1166: 
1167:         local phase=$(get_story_phase "$story_id")
1168:         local phase_info=""
1169:         [ -n "$phase" ] && phase_info=" ${CYAN}[$phase]${NC}"
1170: 
1171:         if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
1172:             echo -e "  ${YELLOW}x${NC} $story_id - $(get_story_title "$story_id")$phase_info [ALREADY COMPLETED]"
1173:         elif ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
1174:             local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
1175:             echo -e "  ${RED}x${NC} $story_id - $(get_story_title "$story_id")$phase_info [BLOCKED: $deps]"
1176:         else
1177:             echo -e "  ${CYAN}$order.${NC} $story_id - $(get_story_title "$story_id")$phase_info"
1178:             order=$((order + 1))
1179:         fi
1180:     done
1181:     echo ""
1182: }
1183: 
1184: # Main implementation loop
1185: run_implementation() {
1186:     local stories=("$@")
1187:     local implemented=0
1188:     local failed=0
1189:     local skipped=0
1190: 
1191:     # If no specific stories provided, get prioritized list
1192:     if [ ${#stories[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
1193:         mapfile -t stories < <(get_prioritized_stories)
1194:     fi
1195: 
1196:     if [ ${#stories[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
1197:         success "All stories are already completed (or blocked by dependencies)!"
1198:         return 0
1199:     fi
1200: 
1201:     log "======================================"
1202:     log "EPAM CLI Orchestration Loop Starting"
1203:     log "Stories to implement: ${stories[*]}"
1204:     log "======================================"
1205: 
1206:     echo ""
1207:     get_project_context
1208:     echo ""
1209: 
1210:     for story_id in "${stories[@]}"; do
1211:         log "--------------------------------------"
1212: 
1213:         # Validate story exists
1214:         if ! story_exists "$story_id"; then
1215:             error "Story $story_id not found in PRD"
1216:             failed=$((failed + 1))
1217:             continue
1218:         fi
1219: 
1220:         # Skip if already completed
1221:         if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
1222:             warning "Story $story_id is already completed, skipping"
1223:             skipped=$((skipped + 1))
1224:             continue
1225:         fi
1226: 
1227:         # Check dependencies using check-dependencies.sh if available
1228:         local dep_checker="$SCRIPT_DIR/check-dependencies.sh"
1229:         if [ -x "$dep_checker" ]; then
1230:             # Use dedicated dependency checker for better validation and output
1231:             if ! PRD_FILE="$PRD_FILE" "$dep_checker" "$story_id" 2>&1; then
1232:                 local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
1233:                 warning "Story $story_id blocked by dependencies: $deps - skipping"
1234:                 log_to_monitor "dependency_blocked" "$story_id" "Blocked by dependencies: $deps"
1235:                 skipped=$((skipped + 1))
1236:                 continue
1237:             fi
1238:         else
1239:             # Fallback to inline dependency check
1240:             if ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
1241:                 local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
1242:                 warning "Story $story_id blocked by dependencies: $deps - skipping"
1243:                 log_to_monitor "dependency_blocked" "$story_id" "Blocked by dependencies: $deps"
1244:                 skipped=$((skipped + 1))
1245:                 continue
1246:             fi
1247:         fi
1248: 
1249:         # Implement the story
1250:         if implement_story "$story_id"; then
1251:             update_story_status "$story_id" "completed"
1252:             implemented=$((implemented + 1))
1253:         else
1254:             update_story_status "$story_id" "failed"
1255:             failed=$((failed + 1))
1256:         fi
1257: 
1258:         increment_iteration
1259:     done
1260: 
1261:     log "======================================"
1262:     log "EPAM CLI Orchestration Loop Complete"
1263:     log "Implemented: $implemented, Failed: $failed, Skipped: $skipped"
1264:     log "======================================"
1265: 
1266:     if [ $failed -gt 0 ]; then
1267:         return 1
1268:     fi
1269:     return 0
1270: }
1271: 
1272: # Setup git worktrees for parallel execution
1273: setup_worktrees() {
1274:     local worktrees=("primary" "independent")
1275: 
1276:     log "Setting up git worktrees..."
1277: 
1278:     for wt in "${worktrees[@]}"; do
1279:         local wt_path="$PROJECT_ROOT/../epam-cli-wt-$wt"
1280:         local wt_branch="wt-$wt"
1281: 
1282:         # Check if worktree already exists
1283:         if [ -d "$wt_path" ]; then
1284:             warning "Worktree already exists: $wt_path"
1285:             continue
1286:         fi
1287: 
1288:         # Delete branch if it exists from previous run
1289:         if git show-ref --verify --quiet "refs/heads/$wt_branch"; then
1290:             info "Deleting existing branch: $wt_branch"
1291:             git branch -D "$wt_branch" 2>/dev/null || true
1292:         fi
1293: 
1294:         # Create worktree with a new branch based on current HEAD
1295:         info "Creating worktree: $wt ($wt_path) on branch $wt_branch"
1296:         git worktree add -b "$wt_branch" "$wt_path" HEAD || {
1297:             error "Failed to create worktree: $wt"
1298:             return 1
1299:         }
1300:     done
1301: 
1302:     success "Worktrees created successfully"
1303:     return 0
1304: }
1305: 
1306: # Cleanup git worktrees
1307: cleanup_worktrees() {
1308:     local worktrees=("primary" "independent")
1309: 
1310:     log "Cleaning up git worktrees..."
1311: 
1312:     for wt in "${worktrees[@]}"; do
1313:         local wt_path="$PROJECT_ROOT/../epam-cli-wt-$wt"
1314: 
1315:         # Check if worktree exists
1316:         if [ ! -d "$wt_path" ]; then
1317:             info "Worktree does not exist: $wt_path (already removed)"
1318:             continue
1319:         fi
1320: 
1321:         # Remove worktree
1322:         info "Removing worktree: $wt ($wt_path)"
1323:         git worktree remove "$wt_path" --force || {
1324:             warning "Failed to remove worktree: $wt (may need manual cleanup)"
1325:         }
1326:     done

# EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
# This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
#
# Usage:
#   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
#   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
#   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
#   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
#   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
#   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
#   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
#
# Note: By default, runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions for autonomous operation.
#       Use --interactive flag if you want to approve each file operation.

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
