<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # Master orchestration script for parallel multi-agent execution
4: # Coordinates worktree-based parallel Claude agents across all EPAM CLI project phases
5: #
6: # Usage:
7: #   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh                                    # Run default phase (finops)
8: #   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops                     # Run specific phase
9: #   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --dry-run                          # Preview execution plan
10: #   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --skip-cleanup                     # Keep worktrees for inspection
11: 
12: set -e
13: 
14: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
15: AUTOMATION_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
16: PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$AUTOMATION_DIR")"
17: PRD_FILE="${PRD_FILE:-$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json}"
18: # Compute PRD path relative to PROJECT_ROOT for injecting into agent prompts
19: PRD_REL="$(realpath --relative-to="$PROJECT_ROOT" "$(realpath "$PRD_FILE")" 2>/dev/null || echo "orchestrations/prd.json")"
20: # Select wrapper script based on CLAUDE_CMD
21: case "${CLAUDE_CMD}" in
22:     codemie-claude) CLAUDE_SH="$SCRIPT_DIR/codemie-claude.sh" ;;
23:     *)              CLAUDE_SH="$SCRIPT_DIR/claude.sh" ;;
24: esac
25: LOG_DIR="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs"
26: MONITOR_STATUS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/agent-status.json"
27: MESSAGES_JSONL="$LOG_DIR/agent-messages.jsonl"
28: CLAUDE_CMD="${CLAUDE_CMD:-claude}"
29: 
30: # Colors
31: RED='\033[0;31m'
32: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
33: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
34: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
35: CYAN='\033[0;36m'
36: MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
37: WHITE='\033[1;37m'
38: NC='\033[0m'
39: 
40: log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[$(date +'%H:%M:%S')]${NC} $1"; }
41: error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1" >&2; }
42: success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
43: info()    { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
44: warning() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"; }
45: 
46: # Default configuration
47: PHASE="finops"
48: DRY_RUN=false
49: SKIP_CLEANUP=false
50: # Orchestration mode: bash (default, no change to existing flow) or hybrid
51: # Override: ORCH_MODE=hybrid ./run-agent-orchestration.sh  OR  --mode hybrid
52: ORCH_MODE="${ORCH_MODE:-bash}"
53: 
54: # Cleanup on exit
55: cleanup() {
56:     local exit_code=$?
57:     if [ "$SKIP_CLEANUP" = "true" ]; then
58:         warning "Skipping worktree cleanup (--skip-cleanup)"
59:         return
60:     fi
61:     if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
62:         error "Execution failed with exit code $exit_code"
63:     fi
64:     log "Cleaning up worktrees..."
65:     "$CLAUDE_SH" --cleanup-worktrees 2>/dev/null || true
66: }
67: 
68: trap cleanup EXIT
69: 
70: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
71: # resolve_orch_mode <phase_id>
72: # Precedence: prd.json phasesConfig[phase].orchestrationMode
73: #             > ORCH_MODE env var > default "bash"
74: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
75: resolve_orch_mode() {
76:     local phase_id="$1"
77:     local phase_mode
78:     phase_mode=$(jq -r \
79:         --arg p "$phase_id" \
80:         '.phasesConfig[$p].orchestrationMode // empty' \
81:         "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)
82:     if [ -n "$phase_mode" ] && [ "$phase_mode" != "null" ]; then
83:         echo "$phase_mode"
84:     else
85:         echo "${ORCH_MODE:-bash}"
86:     fi
87: }
88: 
89: # Parse arguments
90: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
91:     case $1 in
92:         --phase)
93:             if [ -z "$2" ] || [[ "$2" == --* ]]; then
94:                 error "--phase requires a phase name"
95:                 exit 1
96:             fi
97:             PHASE="$2"
98:             shift 2
99:             ;;
100:         --dry-run)
101:             DRY_RUN=true
102:             shift
103:             ;;
104:         --skip-cleanup)
105:             SKIP_CLEANUP=true
106:             shift
107:             ;;
108:         --mode)
109:             if [ -z "${2:-}" ] || [[ "${2:-}" == --* ]]; then
110:                 error "--mode requires a value: bash|hybrid"
111:                 exit 1
112:             fi
113:             if [[ "$2" != "bash" && "$2" != "hybrid" ]]; then
114:                 error "Invalid --mode: $2 (must be 'bash' or 'hybrid')"
115:                 exit 1
116:             fi
117:             ORCH_MODE="$2"
118:             shift 2
119:             ;;
120:         --help|-h)
121:             cat << EOF
122: Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS]
123: 
124: Orchestrates parallel execution of stories using git worktrees.
125: Runs setup stories on main, then launches primary and independent agents
126: in parallel, waits for completion, and runs review.
127: 
128: Options:
129:   --phase NAME        Phase to execute (default: phase_wearables_test)
130:   --mode MODE         Orchestration mode: bash (default) or hybrid
131:   --dry-run           Show execution plan without running
132:   --skip-cleanup      Don't cleanup worktrees on exit (for debugging)
133:   --help              Show this help message
134: 
135: Examples:
136:   $(basename "$0")                                    # Run test phase
137:   $(basename "$0") --phase phase11_wearable_foundation
138:   $(basename "$0") --dry-run                          # Preview plan
139:   $(basename "$0") --skip-cleanup                     # Keep worktrees
140: 
141: EOF
142:             exit 0
143:             ;;
144:         *)
145:             error "Unknown option: $1"
146:             exit 1
147:             ;;
148:     esac
149: done
150: 
151: # Verify prerequisites
152: if [ ! -f "$CLAUDE_SH" ]; then
153:     error "claude.sh not found at $CLAUDE_SH"
154:     exit 1
155: fi
156: if [ ! -f "$PRD_FILE" ]; then
157:     error "prd.json not found at $PRD_FILE"
158:     exit 1
159: fi
160: if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
161:     error "jq is required but not installed"
162:     exit 1
163: fi
164: 
165: # Verify phase exists
166: phase_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" '.implementationOrder[$phase] // empty' "$PRD_FILE")
167: if [ -z "$phase_stories" ] || [ "$phase_stories" = "null" ]; then
168:     error "Phase '$PHASE' not found in prd.json"
169:     echo ""
170:     echo "Available phases:"
171:     jq -r '.implementationOrder | keys[]' "$PRD_FILE" | while read p; do echo "  - $p"; done
172:     exit 1
173: fi
174: 
175: # ── Infra test gate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
176: # Block any phase that depends on infra_test (anything except infra_test itself)
177: # unless all SP-T0x stories are completed.
178: if [ "$PHASE" != "infra_test" ]; then
179:     infra_test_stories=$(jq -r '
180:         (.implementationOrder["infra_test"] // []) as $ids |
181:         .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id))
182:         | .id' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
183: 
184:     if [ -n "$infra_test_stories" ]; then
185:         infra_incomplete=""
186:         while IFS= read -r sid; do
187:             [ -z "$sid" ] && continue
188:             completed=$(jq -r --arg id "$sid" '.stories[] | select(.id==$id) | .completed' "$PRD_FILE")
189:             if [ "$completed" != "true" ]; then
190:                 infra_incomplete="$infra_incomplete $sid"
191:             fi
192:         done <<< "$infra_test_stories"
193: 
194:         if [ -n "$infra_incomplete" ]; then
195:             echo ""
196:             echo -e "${RED}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
197:             echo -e "${RED}║  INFRA TEST GATE — Phase '$PHASE' is BLOCKED         ║${NC}"
198:             echo -e "${RED}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
199:             echo ""
200:             echo -e "${YELLOW}The following infra_test stories must complete before running '$PHASE':${NC}"
201:             for sid in $infra_incomplete; do
202:                 title=$(jq -r --arg id "$sid" '.stories[] | select(.id==$id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE")
203:                 echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} $sid: $title"
204:             done
205:             echo ""
206:             echo -e "${CYAN}Run the infra_test phase first:${NC}"
207:             echo -e "  $(basename "$0") --phase infra_test"
208:             echo ""
209:             echo -e "${YELLOW}If infra_test has been run but status not updated, check:${NC}"
210:             echo -e "  curl -s http://localhost:8090/api/stories | jq '[.[] | select(.phase==\"infra_test\") | {id,status,completed}]'"
211:             echo ""
212:             exit 1
213:         fi
214:     fi
215: fi
216: 
217: # Create log directory
218: mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
219: 
220: # ── Step 0.1: Contextual Purveyor Agent (CPA) pre-pass ───────────────────────
221: # Reviews upcoming phase stories, adjusts estimates, and gates on confidence.
222: # Skip with: SKIP_CPA=1 ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase <phase>
223: # For strict mode (halt on 'review' gate): STRICT_CPA=1
224: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
225: CPA_SCRIPT="$SCRIPT_DIR/contextualize-stories.sh"
226: 
227: if [ "${SKIP_CPA:-0}" != "1" ] && [ -f "$CPA_SCRIPT" ]; then
228:     log "Step 0.1: Running CPA pre-pass for phase '$PHASE'..."
229: 
230:     cpa_flags="--phase $PHASE --apply"
231:     [ "${STRICT_CPA:-0}" = "1" ] && cpa_flags="$cpa_flags --strict"
232: 
233:     cpa_exit=0
234:     # shellcheck disable=SC2086
235:     bash "$CPA_SCRIPT" $cpa_flags 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/cpa-${PHASE}.log" || cpa_exit=$?
236: 
237:     case $cpa_exit in
238:         0)
239:             success "Step 0.1: CPA gate PASSED for phase '$PHASE'"
240:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "cpa_pass" \
241:                 "CPA gate passed — all stories cleared" "" "main" "context-purveyor" 2>/dev/null || true
242:             ;;
243:         2)
244:             warning "Step 0.1: CPA gate REVIEW — some stories have elevated risk"
245:             warning "  Check: $LOG_DIR/cpa-${PHASE}.log"
246:             warning "  Continuing (use STRICT_CPA=1 to halt on review gates)"
247:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "cpa_review" \
248:                 "CPA gate REVIEW — proceeding with warnings" "" "main" "context-purveyor" 2>/dev/null || true
249:             ;;
250:         3)
251:             error "Step 0.1: CPA gate BLOCKED — one or more stories cannot proceed"
252:             error "  Check: $LOG_DIR/cpa-${PHASE}.log"
253:             error "  Resolve flagged issues, then re-run. Override: SKIP_CPA=1"
254:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "cpa_block" \
255:                 "CPA gate BLOCKED — pipeline halted" "" "main" "context-purveyor" 2>/dev/null || true
256:             exit 3
257:             ;;
258:         *)
259:             warning "Step 0.1: CPA script exited with code $cpa_exit (non-critical — continuing)"
260:             ;;
261:     esac
262: else
263:     if [ "${SKIP_CPA:-0}" = "1" ]; then
264:         info "Step 0.1: CPA pre-pass skipped (SKIP_CPA=1)"
265:     else
266:         info "Step 0.1: CPA script not found — skipping pre-pass"
267:     fi
268: fi
269: 
270: # Resolve orchestration mode for this phase (phase config > ORCH_MODE env > bash default)
271: RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE=$(resolve_orch_mode "$PHASE")
272: 
273: echo ""
274: echo -e "${MAGENTA}============================================${NC}"
275: echo -e "${MAGENTA}  EPAM CLI Agent Orchestration${NC}"
276: echo -e "${MAGENTA}  Phase: ${WHITE}$PHASE${NC}"
277: echo -e "${MAGENTA}  Mode:  ${WHITE}$([ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && echo "DRY RUN" || echo "LIVE")${NC}"
278: echo -e "${MAGENTA}  Orch:  ${WHITE}${RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE}$([ "$RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE" = "hybrid" ] && echo " (Agent Teams + MCP bus)" || echo " (bash-only)")${NC}"
279: echo -e "${MAGENTA}============================================${NC}"
280: echo ""
281: 
282: # Categorize stories by agent group
283: main_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
284:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
285:      .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
286:      select(.agentGroup == "main" and .completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
287: 
288: primary_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
289:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
290:      .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
291:      select(.agentGroup == "primary" and .completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
292: 
293: independent_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
294:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
295:      .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
296:      select(.agentGroup == "independent" and .completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
297: 
298: review_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
299:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
300:      .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
301:      select(.agentRole == "review-agent" and .completed == false) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
302: 
303: # Display execution plan
304: echo -e "${CYAN}Execution Plan:${NC}"
305: echo ""
306: if [ -n "$main_stories" ]; then
307:     echo -e "  ${MAGENTA}Main branch (sequential):${NC}"
308:     echo "$main_stories" | while read s; do
309:         [ -z "$s" ] && continue
310:         title=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE")
311:         role=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "none"' "$PRD_FILE")
312:         echo -e "    $s: $title ${CYAN}[$role]${NC}"
313:     done
314:     echo ""
315: fi
316: if [ -n "$primary_stories" ]; then
317:     echo -e "  ${GREEN}Worktree-1 (primary chain):${NC}"
318:     echo "$primary_stories" | while read s; do
319:         [ -z "$s" ] && continue
320:         title=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE")
321:         role=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "none"' "$PRD_FILE")
322:         echo -e "    $s: $title ${GREEN}[$role]${NC}"
323:     done
324:     echo ""
325: fi
326: if [ -n "$independent_stories" ]; then
327:     echo -e "  ${CYAN}Worktree-2 (independent):${NC}"
328:     echo "$independent_stories" | while read s; do
329:         [ -z "$s" ] && continue
330:         title=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE")
331:         role=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "none"' "$PRD_FILE")
332:         echo -e "    $s: $title ${CYAN}[$role]${NC}"
333:     done
334:     echo ""
335: fi
336: if [ -n "$review_stories" ]; then
337:     echo -e "  ${RED}Review (after worktrees complete):${NC}"
338:     echo "$review_stories" | while read s; do
339:         [ -z "$s" ] && continue
340:         title=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE")
341:         echo -e "    $s: $title ${RED}[review-agent]${NC}"
342:     done
343:     echo ""
344: fi
345: 
346: if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
347:     info "Dry run complete. No actions taken."
348:     exit 0
349: fi
350: 
351: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
352: # Initialize monitor status file for HTML dashboard
353: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
354: log "Initializing monitor status file..."
355: 
356: # Build initial stories map from phase
357: stories_init=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
358:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
359:      [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
360:       {key: .id, value: {status: (if .completed then "complete" else "pending" end), lane: .agentGroup, role: (.agentRole // ""), title: .title, updatedAt: null}}] |
361:      from_entries' "$PRD_FILE")
362: 
363: cat > "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" << JSONEOF
364: {
365:   "startedAt": "$(date -Iseconds)",
366:   "phase": "$PHASE",
367:   "orchMode": "$RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE",
368:   "lanes": {
369:     "main": {"status": "idle", "currentStory": null, "storiesCompleted": 0, "storiesFailed": 0},
370:     "primary": {"status": "idle", "currentStory": null, "storiesCompleted": 0, "storiesFailed": 0},
371:     "independent": {"status": "idle", "currentStory": null, "storiesCompleted": 0, "storiesFailed": 0}
372:   },
373:   "events": [],
374:   "stories": $stories_init
375: }
376: JSONEOF
377: 
378: info "Monitor file: $MONITOR_STATUS_FILE"
379: info "Open orchestrations/monitor.html in a browser to watch progress"
380: 
381: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
382: # Step 0.5: Pre-phase skill assessment
383: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
384: run_pre_phase_assessment() {
385:     local phase_id=$1
386:     local profiles_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
387:     local profiles_backup="${profiles_file}.original"
388:     local profiles_audit="$LOG_DIR/profiles-audit.jsonl"
389:     local assessment_log="$LOG_DIR/pre-assessment-${phase_id}.log"
390: 
391:     touch "$profiles_audit"
392: 
393:     # Backup profiles.json on first pre-phase run (idempotent)
394:     if [ ! -f "$profiles_backup" ]; then
395:         cp "$profiles_file" "$profiles_backup"
396:         log "Backed up original profiles to $profiles_backup"
397:     fi
398: 
399:     log "Running pre-phase skill assessment for '$phase_id'..."
400: 
401:     # Build assessment prompt
402:     local assessment_prompt
403:     assessment_prompt=$(cat << PROMPT_HEADER
404: You are the skill assessment agent running in PRE-PHASE mode. Your job is to detect skill gaps in agent profiles BEFORE the phase runs, and augment profiles with missing knowledge.
405: 
406: ## Task
407: 1. Read ${PRD_REL} and find the stories in the current phase's implementationOrder
408: 2. For each story, extract required skills from description + technicalNotes (especially technicalNotes.requiredSkills)
409: 3. Read orchestrations/agents/profiles.json and find the profile for each story's agentRole
410: 4. Compare: does the agent's profile text mention each required skill?
411: 5. For any GAPS found:
412:    a. Append a sentence to the agent's profile in profiles.json mentioning the missing skill
413:    b. Append a JSONL record to orchestrations/logs/profiles-audit.jsonl:
414:       {"timestamp":"<ISO8601>","phase_id":"<phase>","agent_role":"<role>","event":"skill_added","skill":"<skill>","skill_category":"<category>","context":"Story <id> requires <skill>","added_by":"pre-phase-assessment"}
415:    c. Use flock when writing to JSONL files
416: 6. Write a summary to orchestrations/logs/phase-improvements/pre-<phase_id>.md
417: 
418: Known skill categories: deployment_platform, language, framework, testing, database, infrastructure, api, cloud_service
419: 
420: IMPORTANT: Keep profiles.json valid JSON at all times. Only ADD to existing profile strings, never remove content.
421: PROMPT_HEADER
422:     )
423: 
424:     # Append the phase-specific context
425:     assessment_prompt="${assessment_prompt}
426: 
427: ## Phase: ${phase_id}
428: 
429: Read ${PRD_REL} implementationOrder[\"${phase_id}\"] for the story list, then proceed with the analysis above."
430: 
431:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
432:     if echo "$assessment_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$assessment_log"; then
433:         success "Pre-phase assessment completed for '$phase_id'"
434:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "pre_phase_assessment" "Pre-phase assessment completed" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
435:         # Validate profiles.json is still valid JSON
436:         if ! jq empty "$profiles_file" 2>/dev/null; then
437:             error "Pre-phase assessment corrupted profiles.json! Restoring backup."
438:             cp "$profiles_backup" "$profiles_file"
439:             return 1
440:         fi
441:     else
442:         warning "Pre-phase assessment failed for '$phase_id' (non-critical, continuing)"
443:     fi
444: }
445: 
446: log "Step 0.5: Running pre-phase skill assessment..."
447: run_pre_phase_assessment "$PHASE"
448: 
449: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
450: # Step 0.6 (hybrid only): Pre-phase coordination
451: # Seeds the MCP message bus with guidance messages
452: # and identifies any stories requiring plan mode.
453: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
454: run_hybrid_precoordination() {
455:     local phase_id="$1"
456:     local coord_log="$LOG_DIR/hybrid-coord-${phase_id}.log"
457:     local coord_prompt
458:     touch "$MESSAGES_JSONL"
459: 
460:     coord_prompt=$(cat << COORD_EOF
461: You are the coordination agent running in HYBRID PRE-PHASE mode for phase: ${phase_id}.
462: 
463: ## Task
464: 1. Read ${PRD_REL} and locate all stories in implementationOrder["${phase_id}"].
465: 2. Identify cross-lane dependencies between main, primary, and independent agent groups.
466: 3. Flag any stories where estimatedHours >= 6 or dependencies count >= 2 — these require plan mode.
467: 4. For each cross-lane dependency or plan-mode story, append a JSON message to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl.
468:    Use this schema (one compact JSON line per message):
469:    {"id":"coord_<storyid>_<epoch>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>","from_agent":"coordination-agent","to_agent":"<agentRole>","story_id":"<id>","phase_id":"${phase_id}","message_type":"<handoff|plan_required|risk>","priority":"normal","subject":"<subject>","body":"<body>","status":"new"}
470: 5. Post a final {"message_type":"phase_ready","to_agent":"orchestrator","phase_id":"${phase_id}",...} message when complete.
471: 6. Use: (flock -w 10 9 >> orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl; printf '%s\n' '<json>' >&9) 9>>orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl for atomic writes.
472: 7. Write a summary of actions to orchestrations/logs/hybrid-coord-${phase_id}.log.
473: 
474: ## Constraints
475: - Do NOT modify source code or prd.json stories.
476: - Only write to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl and orchestrations/logs/hybrid-coord-${phase_id}.log.
477: COORD_EOF
478:     )
479: 
480:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
481:     if echo "$coord_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$coord_log"; then
482:         success "Hybrid pre-phase coordination completed for '$phase_id'"
483:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "hybrid_precoord" \
484:             "Hybrid pre-phase coordination completed" "" "main" "coordination-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
485:     else
486:         warning "Hybrid pre-phase coordination had issues — continuing with bash fallback"
487:     fi
488: }
489: 
490: if [ "$RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE" = "hybrid" ]; then
491:     log "Step 0.6: Hybrid mode — running pre-phase coordination..."
492:     run_hybrid_precoordination "$PHASE"
493: else
494:     info "Step 0.6: Skipped (ORCH_MODE=${RESOLVED_ORCH_MODE})"
495: fi
496: 
497: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
498: # Step 1: Run main-branch stories (no dependencies, sequential)
499: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
500: if [ -n "$main_stories" ]; then
501:     # Filter out review stories (those run at the end)
502:     non_review_main=$(echo "$main_stories" | while read s; do
503:         [ -z "$s" ] && continue
504:         role=$(jq -r --arg id "$s" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // ""' "$PRD_FILE")
505:         if [ "$role" != "review-agent" ]; then
506:             echo "$s"
507:         fi
508:     done)
509: 
510:     if [ -n "$non_review_main" ]; then
511:         log "Step 1: Running main-branch stories..."
512:         while IFS= read -r story; do
513:             [ -z "$story" ] && continue
514:             log "  Running: $story"
515:             "$CLAUDE_SH" "$story" 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/main-${story}.log"
516:         done <<< "$non_review_main"
517:         success "Main-branch stories complete"
518:     fi
519: else
520:     info "Step 1: No main-branch stories to run"
521: fi
522: 
523: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
524: # Step 2: Create worktrees
525: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
526: need_worktrees=false
527: [ -n "$primary_stories" ] && need_worktrees=true
528: [ -n "$independent_stories" ] && need_worktrees=true
529: 
530: if [ "$need_worktrees" = true ]; then
531:     log "Step 2: Creating git worktrees..."
532:     "$CLAUDE_SH" --setup-worktrees || { error "Failed to create worktrees"; exit 1; }
533: else
534:     info "Step 2: No worktree stories — skipping worktree creation"
535: fi
536: 
537: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
538: # Step 3: Launch parallel agents
539: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
540: PRIMARY_PID=""
541: INDEPENDENT_PID=""
542: 
543: if [ -n "$primary_stories" ]; then
544:     log "Step 3a: Starting primary agent..."
545:     "$CLAUDE_SH" --worktree primary --phase "$PHASE" \
546:         > "$LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log" 2>&1 &
547:     PRIMARY_PID=$!
548:     info "  Primary agent PID: $PRIMARY_PID"
549: fi
550: 
551: if [ -n "$independent_stories" ]; then
552:     log "Step 3b: Starting independent agent..."
553:     "$CLAUDE_SH" --worktree independent --phase "$PHASE" \
554:         > "$LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log" 2>&1 &
555:     INDEPENDENT_PID=$!
556:     info "  Independent agent PID: $INDEPENDENT_PID"
557: fi
558: 
559: # Wait for both agents
560: PRIMARY_EXIT=0
561: INDEPENDENT_EXIT=0
562: 
563: if [ -n "$PRIMARY_PID" ]; then
564:     log "Waiting for primary agent (PID $PRIMARY_PID)..."
565:     wait $PRIMARY_PID || PRIMARY_EXIT=$?
566:     if [ $PRIMARY_EXIT -eq 0 ]; then
567:         success "Primary agent completed successfully"
568:     else
569:         error "Primary agent failed with exit code $PRIMARY_EXIT"
570:         error "Check log: $LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log"
571:     fi
572: fi
573: 
574: if [ -n "$INDEPENDENT_PID" ]; then
575:     log "Waiting for independent agent (PID $INDEPENDENT_PID)..."
576:     wait $INDEPENDENT_PID || INDEPENDENT_EXIT=$?
577:     if [ $INDEPENDENT_EXIT -eq 0 ]; then
578:         success "Independent agent completed successfully"
579:     else
580:         error "Independent agent failed with exit code $INDEPENDENT_EXIT"
581:         error "Check log: $LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log"
582:     fi
583: fi
584: 
585: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
586: # Step 3.1: Worktree health check + auto-commit
587: # Ensures agent-produced code is committed before gate assessment.
588: # Agents sometimes write files without committing (common failure mode).
589: log "Step 3.1: Worktree health check..."
590: PHASE="$PHASE" AUTO_COMMIT=true "$SCRIPT_DIR/worktree-health-check.sh" \
591:     2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/worktree-health-${PHASE}.log" || {
592:     warning "Worktree health check reported issues — see $LOG_DIR/worktree-health-${PHASE}.log"
593:     warning "Continuing (auto-commit attempted) — verify files manually if build fails"
594: }
595: 
596: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
597: # Sync story data to monitor from cost log
598: "$SCRIPT_DIR/sync-monitor-stories.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
599: 
600: # Step 3.5: Post-Parallel Skill Assessment
601: # (Runs immediately after parallel execution; captures mid-pipeline variance.
602: #  Step 6 at end of pipeline performs the final post-phase assessment.)
603: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
604: run_phase_assessment() {
605:     local phase_id=$1
606:     local cost_file="$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl"
607:     local assessment_file="$LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl"
608:     local improvement_dir="$LOG_DIR/phase-improvements"
609: 
610:     mkdir -p "$improvement_dir"
611: 
612:     # Check if phase-cost.jsonl has records for this phase
613:     if [ ! -s "$cost_file" ]; then
614:         warning "No cost records found in $cost_file — skipping assessment"
615:         return 0
616:     fi
617: 
618:     local phase_records=$(grep -c "\"phase_id\":\"$phase_id\"" "$cost_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
619:     if [ "${phase_records:-0}" -eq 0 ]; then
620:         warning "No cost records for phase '$phase_id' — skipping assessment"
621:         return 0
622:     fi
623: 
624:     info "Found $phase_records cost records for phase '$phase_id'"
625: 
626:     # Build assessment prompt
627:     local assessment_prompt
628:     assessment_prompt=$(cat << PROMPT_EOF
629: You are the skill assessment agent. Analyze the phase cost data and produce an assessment.
630: 
631: ## Phase: $phase_id
632: 
633: ## Task
634: 1. Read orchestrations/logs/phase-cost.jsonl and filter for phase_id="$phase_id"
635: 2. For each task, compare elapsed_minutes vs forecast_hours (converted to minutes)
636: 3. Calculate phase-level totals and variance
637: 4. Write a single-line JSON assessment to orchestrations/logs/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl with fields:
638:    phase_id, phase_name, actual_minutes, forecast_minutes, actual_cost_usd, forecast_cost_usd,
639:    variance_minutes, variance_cost_usd, over_threshold (bool), agent_recommendations (array), notes
640: 5. Write a human-readable improvement report to orchestrations/logs/phase-improvements/${phase_id}.md
641: 6. CORRECTIVE ACTION: If any task's description clearly requires a different skill domain than the assigned agentRole,
642:    update ${PRD_REL} to change agentRole for FUTURE phase stories that have the same mismatch.
643:    - Only modify stories that are status "pending" and completed false
644:    - Only modify stories in phases AFTER the current phase (do NOT modify completed phase stories)
645:    - When changing agentRole, preserve the original value in the "originalAgentRole" field (already present)
646:    - Document every role change in the improvement report
647:    - Skill domain indicators: "TypeScript" / "Node.js" / "CLI" → backend-engineer,
648:      "React" / "UI" / "frontend" → frontend-engineer, "Docker" / "infrastructure" → devops-engineer,
649:      "Vitest" / "testing" / "E2E" → qa-engineer
650: 
651: Use flock when appending to JSONL files. If all tasks were within forecast, note "No improvements needed."
652: PROMPT_EOF
653:     )
654: 
655:     log "Running assessment agent for phase '$phase_id'..."
656:     local assessment_log="$LOG_DIR/assessment-${phase_id}.log"
657: 
658:     # Backup prd.json before assessment modifies it
659:     cp "$PRD_FILE" "${PRD_FILE}.pre-assessment"
660:     log "Backed up prd.json to ${PRD_FILE}.pre-assessment"
661: 
662:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
663:     if echo "$assessment_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$assessment_log"; then
664:         success "Phase assessment completed for '$phase_id'"
665:     else
666:         warning "Phase assessment failed for '$phase_id' (non-critical)"
667:     fi
668: }
669: 
670: # Only run assessment if cost tracking data exists
671: if [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl" ]; then
672:     log "Step 3.5: Running post-parallel skill assessment..."
673:     run_phase_assessment "$PHASE"
674: else
675:     info "Step 3.5: No cost data yet — skipping post-parallel assessment"
676: fi
677: 
678: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
679:     "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_assessment" "Running post-phase assessment" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
680: # Step 3.6: Team Lead Code Review
681: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
682: log "Step 3.6: Running Team Lead code review for phase..."
683: if "$SCRIPT_DIR/team-lead-review.sh" "$PHASE"; then
684:     success "Team Lead code review completed for phase '$PHASE'"
685: else
686:     warning "Team Lead code review had issues (check logs)"
687: fi
688: 
689: # Hard-block if any story was escalated (max iterations exhausted without approval)
690: _escalated=$(jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
691:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
692:      [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id) != null) |
693:       select(.reviewStatus == "escalated")] | length' \
694:     "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
695: if [ "${_escalated:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
696:     error "Step 3.6: $_escalated escalated story/stories — max review iterations exhausted without approval"
697:     error "         Human review required before pipeline can proceed"
698:     exit 2
699: fi
700: 
701: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
702: # Step 4: Run review stories
703: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
704: if [ -n "$review_stories" ]; then
705:     log "Step 4: Running review stories..."
706:     while IFS= read -r story; do
707:         [ -z "$story" ] && continue
708:     "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "code_review" "Team Lead code review completed" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
709:         log "  Running review: $story"
710:         "$CLAUDE_SH" "$story" 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/review-${story}.log"
711:     done <<< "$review_stories"
712:     success "Review stories complete"
713: else
714:     info "Step 4: No review stories in this phase"
715: fi
716: 
717: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
718: # run_unit_tests_gate <phase_id>
719: # Step 4.5: Independent unit test verification.
720: # Runs vitest (unit tests) and tsc --noEmit (type check) directly.
721: # Blocks phase gate if any suite fails. Skippable with SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true.
722: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
723: run_unit_tests_gate() {
724:     local phase_id="$1"
725:     local gate_log="$LOG_DIR/unit-test-gate-${phase_id}.log"
726:     local failed=0
727: 
728:     if [ "${SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE:-false}" = "true" ]; then
729:         info "Step 4.5: Unit test gate skipped (SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true)"
730:         return 0
731:     fi
732: 
733:     local phase_has_unit_tests
734:     phase_has_unit_tests=$(jq -r --arg phase "$phase_id" \
735:         '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
736:          [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) | select(.unitTests == true)] | length' \
737:         "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
738:     if [ "${phase_has_unit_tests:-0}" -eq 0 ]; then
739:         info "Step 4.5: No unit-test stories in phase '$phase_id' — skipping unit test gate"
740:         return 0
741:     fi
742: 
743:     log "Step 4.5: Independent unit test gate for '$phase_id'..."
744:     echo "=== Unit Test Gate: $phase_id @ $(date -Iseconds) ===" > "$gate_log"
745: 
746:     # Node.js project: run vitest + tsc
747:     if [ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/package.json" ]; then
748:         log "  Running unit tests (vitest)..."
749:         if ~/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
750:                 2>&1 | tee -a "$gate_log"; then
751:             success "  Unit tests passed (vitest)"
752:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_pass" \
753:                 "Unit tests passed (vitest)" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
754:         else
755:             error "  Unit tests FAILED (vitest)"
756:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_fail" \
757:                 "Unit tests FAILED (vitest) — blocking phase gate" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
758:             failed=1
759:         fi
760: 
761:         log "  Running type check (tsc --noEmit)..."
762:         if ~/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit \
763:                 2>&1 | tee -a "$gate_log"; then
764:             success "  Type check passed (tsc)"
765:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_pass" \
766:                 "Type check passed (tsc)" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
767:         else
768:             error "  Type check FAILED (tsc)"
769:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_fail" \
770:                 "Type check FAILED (tsc) — blocking phase gate" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
771:             failed=1
772:         fi
773:     else
774:         info "  No package.json at PROJECT_ROOT — skipping vitest/tsc"
775:     fi
776: 
777:     echo "=== Gate Result: $([ $failed -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || echo FAIL) ===" >> "$gate_log"
778: 
779:     if [ $failed -ne 0 ]; then
780:         error "Unit test gate FAILED — fix failures and re-run this phase"
781:         error "Bypass (for non-code phases): SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true $0 --phase $phase_id"
782:         error "Log: $gate_log"
783:         return 1
784:     fi
785: 
786:     success "Unit test gate PASSED"
787:     return 0
788: }
789: 
790: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
791: # run_interstitial_e2e_phase <phase_id>
792: # Step 5.5: After phase gate passes, check for a <phase_id>_e2e phase
793: # in implementationOrder and run it. Blocks next phase if E2E fails.
794: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
795: run_interstitial_e2e_phase() {
796:     local phase_id="$1"
797:     local e2e_phase="${phase_id}_e2e"
798: 
799:     local has_e2e_phase
800:     has_e2e_phase=$(jq -r --arg p "$e2e_phase" \
801:         'if .implementationOrder[$p] then "yes" else "no" end' \
802:         "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "no")
803: 
804:     if [ "$has_e2e_phase" = "no" ]; then
805:         info "Step 5.5: No interstitial E2E phase for '$phase_id' — skipping"
806:         return 0
807:     fi
808: 
809:     log "Step 5.5: Running interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase'..."
810:     "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_start" \
811:         "Starting E2E phase $e2e_phase" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
812: 
813:     local e2e_log="$LOG_DIR/e2e-phase-${e2e_phase}.log"
814:     if bash "$0" --phase "$e2e_phase" 2>&1 | tee "$e2e_log"; then
815:         success "Interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase' PASSED"
816:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_pass" \
817:             "E2E phase $e2e_phase passed" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
818:     else
819:         local e2e_exit=$?
820:         error "Interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase' FAILED (exit $e2e_exit)"
821:         error "Fix E2E failures then re-run: $0 --phase $e2e_phase"
822:         error "Log: $e2e_log"
823:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_fail" \
824:             "E2E phase $e2e_phase FAILED" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
825:         return 1
826:     fi
827: }
828: 
829: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
830: # Step 4.5: Unit test gate
831: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
832: run_unit_tests_gate "$PHASE"
833: 
834: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
835: # Step 4.8: Pre-gate worktree health verification
836: # Second chance to catch uncommitted files before gate assessment.
837: # (Step 3.1 auto-commits; this surfaces any residual issues clearly.)
838: log "Step 4.8: Pre-gate worktree verification..."
839: if ! PHASE="$PHASE" "$SCRIPT_DIR/worktree-health-check.sh" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
840:     warning "Step 4.8: Uncommitted files remain in worktrees after auto-commit — manual review recommended"
841:     warning "  Run: PHASE=$PHASE AUTO_COMMIT=true $SCRIPT_DIR/worktree-health-check.sh"
842: fi
843: 
844: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
845: # Step 5: Check phase gate
846: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
847: log "Step 5: Checking phase gate..."
848: "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_check" "Checking phase gate for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
849: 
850: # Run phase gate check (skip tests for now - future enhancement)
851: gate_result=0
852: SKIP_TESTS=true "$SCRIPT_DIR/check-phase-gate.sh" "$PHASE" 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log" || gate_result=$?
853: 
854: case $gate_result in
855:     0)
856:         success "Phase gate: GO - All criteria passed"
857:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_pass" "Phase gate passed for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
858:         # Step 5.5: Interstitial E2E phase (runs <PHASE>_e2e if it exists)
859:         run_interstitial_e2e_phase "$PHASE"
860:         ;;
861:     1)
862:         warning "Phase gate: RETRY - Issues found but fixable"
863:         warning "Check log for details: $LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log"
864:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_retry" "Phase gate requires retry for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
865:         error "Pipeline blocked — fix issues then re-run this phase"
866:         exit 1
867:         ;;
868:     2)
869:         error "Phase gate: ESCALATE - Variance exceeds GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD (${GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD:-150}%)"
870:         error "Check log for details: $LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log"
871:         error "Override: GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD=200 $0 --phase NEXT_PHASE"
872:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_escalate" "Phase gate requires escalation for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
873:         exit 2
874:         ;;
875: esac
876: 
877: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
878: # Step 6: Final Post-Phase Assessment
879: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
880: log "Step 6: Running final post-phase assessment..."
881: if [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl" ]; then
882:     run_phase_assessment "$PHASE"
883: else
884:     info "Step 6: No cost data — skipping final post-phase assessment"
885: fi
886: 
887: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
888: # Step 7: Load Phase Graph into Neo4j
889: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
890: LOAD_GRAPH_SH="$SCRIPT_DIR/load-phase-graph.sh"
891: if [ -f "$LOAD_GRAPH_SH" ]; then
892:     log "Step 7: Loading phase graph into Neo4j..."
893:     if PHASE="$PHASE" bash "$LOAD_GRAPH_SH" --phase "$PHASE" >> "$LOG_DIR/neo4j-import.log" 2>&1; then
894:         success "Step 7: Phase graph loaded — Bloom: http://localhost:7474/browser/bloom"
895:     else
896:         warning "Step 7: Neo4j graph load skipped (Neo4j may not be running)"
897:     fi
898: fi
899: 
900: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
901: # Summary
902: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
903: echo ""
904: echo -e "${MAGENTA}============================================${NC}"
905: echo -e "${MAGENTA}  Orchestration Complete${NC}"
906: echo -e "${MAGENTA}============================================${NC}"
907: echo ""
908: 
909: # Show final story status for this phase
910: jq -r --arg phase "$PHASE" \
911:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
912:      .stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
913:      "\(if .completed then "  ✓" else "  ○" end) \(.id): \(.title) [\(.status // "pending")]"' \
914:     "$PRD_FILE"
915: 
916: echo ""
917: 
918: # Finalize monitor
919: "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" finalize 2>/dev/null || true
920: log "Log files:"
921: [ -f "$LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log" ] && info "  Primary:     $LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log"
922: [ -f "$LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log" ] && info "  Independent: $LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log"
923: info "  Claude outputs: $LOG_DIR/claude_outputs/"
924: info "  Monitor:     $MONITOR_STATUS_FILE"
925: [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl" ] && info "  Phase costs: $LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl"
926: [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl" ] && info "  Assessments: $LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl"
927: 
928: # Mark orchestration complete in monitor file
929: if [ -f "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" ]; then
930:     jq --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
931:         '.completedAt = $ts | .events += [{"type": "orchestration_complete", "story": "", "lane": "main", "role": "", "message": "All steps finished", "timestamp": $ts}]' \
932:         "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" > "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE.tmp" && mv "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE.tmp" "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE"
933: fi
934: 
935: # Exit with error if any agent failed
936: if [ $PRIMARY_EXIT -ne 0 ] || [ $INDEPENDENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
937:     exit 1
938: fi

(End of file - total 938 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
#!/bin/bash

# Master orchestration script for parallel multi-agent execution
# Coordinates worktree-based parallel Claude agents across all EPAM CLI project phases
#
# Usage:
#   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh                                    # Run default phase (finops)
#   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops                     # Run specific phase
#   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --dry-run                          # Preview execution plan
#   ./run-agent-orchestration.sh --skip-cleanup                     # Keep worktrees for inspection

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
AUTOMATION_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$AUTOMATION_DIR")"
PRD_FILE="${PRD_FILE:-$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json}"
# Compute PRD path relative to PROJECT_ROOT for injecting into agent prompts
PRD_REL="$(realpath --relative-to="$PROJECT_ROOT" "$(realpath "$PRD_FILE")" 2>/dev/null || echo "orchestrations/prd.json")"
# Select wrapper script based on CLAUDE_CMD

state.output:
<path>/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/orchestrations/agents/KB.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Shared Knowledge Base
2: 
3: Shared context available to all agents during orchestrated execution.
4: 
5: ## Project: epam-cli
6: 
7: - **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
8: - **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
9: - **Package manager**: npm
10: - **Test framework**: vitest
11: - **Build tool**: tsup
12: - **Linter**: eslint + prettier
13: 
14: ## Key Paths
15: 
16: | Path | Purpose |
17: |------|---------|
18: | `src/index.ts` | CLI entry point |
19: | `src/cli/` | Commander commands, REPL, slash commands |
20: | `src/providers/` | LLM provider adapters (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) |
21: | `src/agent/` | ReAct agent loop |
22: | `src/tools/` | Built-in tools (ReadFile, WriteFile, Bash, Search, etc.) |
23: | `src/auth/` | OAuth device flow, token management |
24: | `src/config/` | Config resolver (global → project → env → flags) |
25: | `src/billing/` | Tier detection, BYOK key store, provider selection |
26: | `src/context/` | Session store, context loader, memory compressor |
27: | `test/` | Unit and integration tests |
28: 
29: ## Conventions
30: 
31: - All source in `src/`, tests in `test/unit/` mirroring src structure
32: - Types defined in `types.ts` per module directory
33: - Errors extend `EpamError` from `src/utils/errors.ts`
34: - Config hierarchy: CLI flags > EPAM_* env vars > .epam/settings.json > ~/.epam/config.json > defaults
35: - Provider chain: up to 5 LLM slots with automatic failover (circuit breaker pattern)
36: 
37: ## Test Gate
38: 
39: ```bash
40: # Must pass before phase gate approval
41: npx vitest run          # unit + integration tests
42: npx tsc --noEmit        # TypeScript strict check
43: ```
44: 
45: ## Auth Model
46: 
47: - **Free tier**: BYOK (bring your own key) — direct provider calls
48: - **Pro/Enterprise**: Proxy through backend-stub — JWT claims carry tier info
49: - **Device flow** (RFC 8628) is default auth; browser PKCE optional with `--browser`

# Shared Knowledge Base

Shared context available to all agents during orchestrated execution.

## Project: epam-cli

- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Package manager**: npm
- **Test framework**: vitest
- **Build tool**: tsup
- **Linter**: eslint + prettier

## Key Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/index.ts` | CLI entry point |
| `src/cli/` | Commander commands, REPL, slash commands |
| `src/providers/` | LLM provider adapters (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) |

1334: 
1335: # Print usage
1336: usage() {
1337:     cat << EOF
1338: Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS] [STORY_IDS...]
1339: 
1340: EPAM CLI Orchestration script for implementing PRD stories.
1341: 
1342: Options:
1343:   --status              Show current PRD status with phase information
1344:   --list-phases         Show all implementation phases and their stories
1345:   --phase NAME          Implement all incomplete stories in a specific phase
1346:   --worktree NAME       Run in worktree mode (primary|independent)
1347:   --setup-worktrees     Create git worktrees for parallel execution
1348:   --cleanup-worktrees   Remove git worktrees
1349:   --dry-run             Show what would be implemented without running
1350:   --interactive         Run with permission prompts (safer, requires approval)
1351:   --help                Show this help message
1352: 
1353: Arguments:
1354:   STORY_IDS       Specific story IDs to implement (e.g., US-001 US-002)
1355:                   If not provided, implements stories in priority order
1356: 
1357: Story Prioritization:
1358:   Stories are implemented based on:
1359:   1. Phase order (from implementationOrder in prd.json)
1360:   2. Dependency satisfaction (blocked stories are skipped)
1361:   3. Priority field (high > medium > low)
1362: 
1363: Environment Variables:
1364:   CLAUDE_CMD      Path to Claude CLI (default: claude)
1365: 
1366: Permissions:
1367:   By default, the script runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions to allow
1368:   autonomous file read/write operations. Use --interactive if you want to
1369:   manually approve each operation.
1370: 
1371: Examples:
1372:   $(basename "$0")                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
1373:   $(basename "$0") --phase phase1       # Implement phase1 stories only
1374:   $(basename "$0") --list-phases        # Show all phases and progress
1375:   $(basename "$0") US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
1376:   $(basename "$0") --dry-run            # Preview implementation order
1377:   $(basename "$0") --status             # Show PRD status
1378:   $(basename "$0") --interactive        # Run with manual approval prompts
1379: 
1380: EOF
1381: }
1382: 
1383: # Main entry point
1384: main() {
1385:     local dry_run_mode=false
1386:     local status_mode=false
1387:     local list_phases_mode=false
1388:     local interactive_mode=false
1389:     local phase_filter=""
1390:     local stories=()
1391: 
1392:     # Parse arguments
1393:     while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
1394:         case $1 in
1395:             --help|-h)
1396:                 usage
1397:                 exit 0
1398:                 ;;
1399:             --status)
1400:                 status_mode=true
1401:                 shift
1402:                 ;;
1403:             --list-phases)
1404:                 list_phases_mode=true
1405:                 shift
1406:                 ;;
1407:             --phase)
1408:                 if [ -z "$2" ] || [[ "$2" == --* ]]; then
1409:                     error "--phase requires a phase name"
1410:                     exit 1
1411:                 fi
1412:                 phase_filter="$2"
1413:                 shift 2
1414:                 ;;
1415:             --dry-run)
1416:                 dry_run_mode=true
1417:                 shift
1418:                 ;;
1419:             --worktree)
1420:                 if [ -z "$2" ] || [[ "$2" == --* ]]; then
1421:                     error "--worktree requires a worktree name (primary|independent)"
1422:                     exit 1
1423:                 fi
1424:                 if [[ "$2" != "primary" && "$2" != "independent" ]]; then
1425:                     error "Invalid worktree name: $2 (must be 'primary' or 'independent')"
1426:                     exit 1
1427:                 fi
1428:                 WORKTREE_MODE="$2"
1429:                 # Save main PRD location for reference
1430:                 MAIN_PRD_FILE="$PRD_FILE"
1431:                 # Update PROJECT_ROOT to worktree for file operations
1432:                 PROJECT_ROOT="$PROJECT_ROOT/../epam-cli-wt-$WORKTREE_MODE"
1433:                 # Keep PRD_FILE pointing to MAIN - single source of truth
1434:                 # (Do NOT set PRD_FILE to worktree's prd.json - it will be stale)
1435:                 shift 2
1436:                 ;;
1437:             --setup-worktrees)
1438:                 initialize
1439:                 check_prerequisites
1440:                 setup_worktrees
1441:                 exit $?
1442:                 ;;
1443:             --cleanup-worktrees)
1444:                 cleanup_worktrees
1445:                 exit $?
1446:                 ;;
1447:             --interactive)
1448:                 interactive_mode=true
1449:                 shift
1450:                 ;;
1451:             -*)
1452:                 error "Unknown option: $1"
1453:                 usage
1454:                 exit 1
1455:                 ;;
1456:             *)
1457:                 stories+=("$1")
1458:                 shift
1459:                 ;;
1460:         esac
1461:     done
1462: 
1463:     # If interactive mode, clear the permission flags
1464:     if [ "$interactive_mode" = true ]; then
1465:         CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=()
1466:         warning "Running in interactive mode - you will be prompted for permissions"
1467:     fi
1468: 
1469:     # Initialize
1470:     initialize
1471:     check_prerequisites
1472: 
1473:     # Execute requested mode
1474:     if [ "$status_mode" = true ]; then
1475:         show_status
1476:         exit 0
1477:     fi
1478: 
1479:     if [ "$list_phases_mode" = true ]; then
1480:         list_phases
1481:         exit 0
1482:     fi
1483: 
1484:     # If phase filter specified, get stories for that phase
1485:     if [ -n "$phase_filter" ]; then
1486:         local phase_stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase_filter")
1487:         if [ -z "$phase_stories" ]; then
1488:             error "Phase '$phase_filter' not found or has no stories"
1489:             echo ""
1490:             echo "Available phases:"
1491:             get_phases | while read p; do echo "  - $p"; done
1492:             exit 1
1493:         fi
1494: 
1495:         # When in worktree mode, filter phase stories by agent group
1496:         if [ -n "$WORKTREE_MODE" ]; then
1497:             local filtered_stories=()
1498:             while IFS= read -r sid; do
1499:                 [ -z "$sid" ] && continue
1500:                 local story_group=$(jq -r --arg id "$sid" \
1501:                     '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentGroup // "main"' "$PRD_FILE")
1502:                 if [ "$story_group" = "$WORKTREE_MODE" ]; then
1503:                     filtered_stories+=("$sid")
1504:                 fi
1505:             done <<< "$phase_stories"
1506:             stories=("${filtered_stories[@]}")
1507:             info "Filtered to ${#stories[@]} stories for agent group: $WORKTREE_MODE"
1508:         else
1509:             mapfile -t stories < <(echo "$phase_stories")
1510:         fi
1511:         CURRENT_PHASE="$phase_filter"
1512:         info "Running phase: $phase_filter"
1513: 
1514:         # Initialize/update monitor status file for this phase, merging with existing stories
1515:         local existing_phase
1516:         existing_phase=$(jq -r '.phase // ""' "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
1517:         if [ ! -f "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" ] || [ "$existing_phase" != "$phase_filter" ]; then
1518:             local new_stories
1519:             new_stories=$(jq -r --arg phase "$phase_filter" \
1520:                 '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
1521:                  [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) |
1522:                   {key: .id, value: {status: (if .completed then "complete" else "pending" end),
1523:                    lane: (.agentGroup // "main"), role: (.agentRole // ""),
1524:                    title: .title, updatedAt: null}}] |
1525:                  from_entries' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
1526:             local orch_mode
1527:             orch_mode=$(jq -r --arg ph "$phase_filter" '.phasesConfig[$ph].orchestrationMode // "bash"' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "bash")
1528:             # Merge: keep existing stories, add new phase stories on top
1529:             local existing_stories='{}'
1530:             if [ -f "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" ]; then
1531:                 existing_stories=$(jq -r '.stories // {}' "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
1532:             fi
1533:             local merged_stories
1534:             merged_stories=$(jq -n --argjson existing "$existing_stories" --argjson new "$new_stories" \
1535:                 '$existing * $new')
1536:             local tmp_init
1537:             tmp_init=$(mktemp "${MONITOR_STATUS_FILE}.init.XXXXXX")
1538:             jq -n \
1539:                 --arg started "$(date -Iseconds)" \
1540:                 --arg phase "$phase_filter" \
1541:                 --arg mode "$orch_mode" \
1542:                 --argjson stories "$merged_stories" \
1543:                 '{startedAt: $started, phase: $phase, orchMode: $mode,
1544:                   lanes: {
1545:                     main:        {status:"idle",currentStory:null,storiesCompleted:0,storiesFailed:0},
1546:                     primary:     {status:"idle",currentStory:null,storiesCompleted:0,storiesFailed:0},
1547:                     independent: {status:"idle",currentStory:null,storiesCompleted:0,storiesFailed:0}
1548:                   },
1549:                   events: [], stories: $stories}' > "$tmp_init" && mv "$tmp_init" "$MONITOR_STATUS_FILE"
1550:             info "Monitor status file updated for phase: $phase_filter"
1551:         fi
1552:     fi
1553: 
1554:     if [ "$dry_run_mode" = true ]; then
1555:         dry_run "${stories[@]}"
1556:         exit 0
1557:     fi
1558: 
1559:     # Step 0.5: Pre-phase skill assessment (main process only, not worktree subprocesses)
1560:     [ -z "$WORKTREE_MODE" ] && run_pre_phase_assessment "$phase_filter"
1561: 
1562:     # -- Parallel lane execution --
1563:     # When not already in worktree mode, partition stories by agentGroup.
1564:     # main stories run sequentially first; primary + independent launch in parallel.
1565:     if [ -z "$WORKTREE_MODE" ] && [ -n "$phase_filter" ]; then
1566:         local main_stories=() primary_stories=() independent_stories=()
1567:         for sid in "${stories[@]}"; do
1568:             local grp
1569:             grp=$(jq -r --arg id "$sid" \
1570:                 '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentGroup // "main"' "$PRD_FILE")
1571:             case "$grp" in
1572:                 primary)     primary_stories+=("$sid") ;;
1573:                 independent) independent_stories+=("$sid") ;;
1574:                 *)           main_stories+=("$sid") ;;
1575:             esac
1576:         done
1577: 
1578:         # Run main-lane stories sequentially first
1579:         if [ ${#main_stories[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
1580:             info "Running ${#main_stories[@]} main-lane stories sequentially..."
1581:             run_implementation "${main_stories[@]}"
1582:         fi
1583: 
1584:         # If there are worktree-lane stories, set up worktrees and launch in parallel
1585:         local need_worktrees=false
1586:         [ ${#primary_stories[@]} -gt 0 ]     && need_worktrees=true
1587:         [ ${#independent_stories[@]} -gt 0 ] && need_worktrees=true
1588: 
1589:         if [ "$need_worktrees" = true ]; then
1590:             info "Setting up git worktrees for parallel execution..."
1591:             setup_worktrees || warning "Worktree setup had errors — continuing"
1592: 
1593:             local PRIMARY_PID="" INDEPENDENT_PID=""
1594:             local SCRIPT_PATH
1595:             SCRIPT_PATH="$(realpath "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")"
1596: 
1597:             if [ ${#primary_stories[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
1598:                 info "Launching primary lane (${#primary_stories[@]} stories) in background..."
1599:                 PRD_FILE="$PRD_FILE" "$SCRIPT_PATH" --worktree primary --phase "$phase_filter" \
1600:                     > "$LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log" 2>&1 &
1601:                 PRIMARY_PID=$!
1602:                 info "  Primary agent PID: $PRIMARY_PID"
1603:             fi
1604: 
1605:             if [ ${#independent_stories[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
1606:                 info "Launching independent lane (${#independent_stories[@]} stories) in background..."
1607:                 PRD_FILE="$PRD_FILE" "$SCRIPT_PATH" --worktree independent --phase "$phase_filter" \
1608:                     > "$LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log" 2>&1 &
1609:                 INDEPENDENT_PID=$!
1610:                 info "  Independent agent PID: $INDEPENDENT_PID"
1611:             fi
1612: 
1613:             # Wait for parallel lanes to finish
1614:             local primary_exit=0 independent_exit=0
1615:             if [ -n "$PRIMARY_PID" ]; then
1616:                 wait "$PRIMARY_PID" || primary_exit=$?
1617:                 [ $primary_exit -eq 0 ] \
1618:                     && success "Primary lane completed" \
1619:                     || warning "Primary lane exited with code $primary_exit — see $LOG_DIR/wt-primary.log"
1620:             fi
1621:             if [ -n "$INDEPENDENT_PID" ]; then
1622:                 wait "$INDEPENDENT_PID" || independent_exit=$?
1623:                 [ $independent_exit -eq 0 ] \
1624:                     && success "Independent lane completed" \
1625:                     || warning "Independent lane exited with code $independent_exit — see $LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log"
1626:             fi
1627:         fi
1628: 
1629:         return 0
1630:     fi
1631: 
1632:     # Fallback: no phase filter or already in worktree mode — run sequentially
1633:     run_implementation "${stories[@]}"
1634: }
1635: 
1636: run_pre_phase_assessment() {
1637:     local phase_id=$1
1638:     local profiles_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
1639:     local profiles_backup="${profiles_file}.original"
1640:     local profiles_audit="$LOG_DIR/profiles-audit.jsonl"
1641:     local assessment_log="$LOG_DIR/pre-assessment-${phase_id}.log"
1642: 
1643:     touch "$profiles_audit"
1644: 
1645:     if [ ! -f "$profiles_backup" ]; then
1646:         cp "$profiles_file" "$profiles_backup"
1647:         info "Backed up original profiles to $profiles_backup"
1648:     fi
1649: 
1650:     info "Running pre-phase skill assessment for '$phase_id'..."
1651: 
1652:     local prd_rel
1653:     prd_rel=$(realpath --relative-to="$PROJECT_ROOT" "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "orchestrations/prd.json")
1654: 
1655:     local assessment_prompt
1656:     assessment_prompt=$(cat << PROMPT_HEADER
1657: You are the skill assessment agent running in PRE-PHASE mode. Your job is to detect skill gaps in agent profiles BEFORE the phase runs, and augment profiles with missing knowledge.
1658: 
1659: ## Task
1660: 1. Read ${prd_rel} and find the stories in the current phase's implementationOrder
1661: 2. For each story, extract required skills from description + technicalNotes (especially technicalNotes.requiredSkills)
1662: 3. Read orchestrations/agents/profiles.json and find the profile for each story's agentRole
1663: 4. Compare: does the agent's profile text mention each required skill?
1664: 5. For any GAPS found:
1665:    a. Append a sentence to the agent's profile in profiles.json mentioning the missing skill
1666:    b. Append a JSONL record to orchestrations/logs/profiles-audit.jsonl:
1667:       {"timestamp":"<ISO8601>","phase_id":"<phase>","agent_role":"<role>","event":"skill_added","skill":"<skill>","skill_category":"<category>","context":"Story <id> requires <skill>","added_by":"pre-phase-assessment"}
1668:    c. Use flock when writing to JSONL files
1669: 6. Write a summary to orchestrations/logs/phase-improvements/pre-${phase_id}.md
1670: 
1671: Known skill categories: deployment_platform, language, framework, testing, database, infrastructure, api, cloud_service
1672: 
1673: IMPORTANT: Keep profiles.json valid JSON at all times. Only ADD to existing profile strings, never remove content.
1674: 
1675: ## Phase: ${phase_id}
1676: 
1677: Read ${prd_rel} implementationOrder["${phase_id}"] for the story list, then proceed with the analysis above.
1678: PROMPT_HEADER
1679:     )
1680: 
1681:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
1682:     if echo "$assessment_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$assessment_log"; then
1683:         success "Pre-phase assessment completed for '$phase_id'"
1684:         if ! jq empty "$profiles_file" 2>/dev/null; then
1685:             warning "Pre-phase assessment may have corrupted profiles.json! Restoring backup."
1686:             cp "$profiles_backup" "$profiles_file"
1687:         fi
1688:     else
1689:         warning "Pre-phase assessment failed for '$phase_id' (non-critical, continuing)"
1690:     fi
1691: }
1692: 
1693: # Run main
1694: main "$@"

(End of file - total 1694 lines)
</content>

files.before:
#!/bin/bash

# EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
# This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
#
# Usage:
#   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
#   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
#   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
#   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
#   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
#   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
#   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
#
# Note: By default, runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions for autonomous operation.
#       Use --interactive flag if you want to approve each file operation.

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
AUTOMATION_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$AUTOMATION_DIR")"
PRD_FILE="${PRD_FILE:-$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json}"
LOG_DIR="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs"
PROGRESS_LOG="$LOG_DIR/progress.txt"
AGENTS_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/AGENTS.md"
CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR="$LOG_DIR/claude_outputs"
AGENT_PROFILES_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
MONITOR_STATUS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/agent-status.json"

# Worktree configuration (set by --worktree flag)
WORKTREE_MODE=""        # "primary", "independent", or "" for main
MAIN_PRD_FILE=""        # Points to main repo's prd.json when in worktree mode
REVIEW_PHASE=""         # Phase name for --review-phase mode
CURRENT_PHASE=""        # Current phase being executed (for cost tracking)

# Configuration
CLAUDE_CMD="${CLAUDE_CMD:-claude}"  # Allow override via environment
MAX_RETRIES=2
RETRY_DELAY=5
# Orchestration mode — inherited from run-agent-orchestration.sh or set directly
ORCH_MODE="${ORCH_MODE:-bash}"

# Effort -> model + max-turns mapping
# Stories carry an optional "effort" field: low | medium (default) | high
# These map to a model and a max-turns cap for the Claude CLI invocation.
EFFORT_MODEL_LOW="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH="claude-opus-4-6"

# resolve_effort_settings <story_id>
# Sets STORY_MODEL and STORY_MAX_TURNS globals based on story's effort field.
resolve_effort_settings() {
    local story_id="$1"
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local effort
    effort=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .effort // "medium"' \
        "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "medium")

    case "$effort" in
        low)
            STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_LOW"
            STORY_MAX_TURNS=10
            ;;
        high)
            STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH"
            STORY_MAX_TURNS=""
            ;;
        *)  # medium (default)
            STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM"
            STORY_MAX_TURNS=30
            ;;
    esac
    log "  Effort[$effort] -> model=$(basename $STORY_MODEL) turns=${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-unlimited}"
}

# resolve_provider_settings <story_id>
# Reads aiProvider from the story and sets STORY_PROVIDER global.
# Values: claude-sonnet | claude-opus | opencode | codex | epam (default: claude-sonnet)
resolve_provider_settings() {
    local story_id="$1"
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    STORY_PROVIDER=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .aiProvider // "claude-sonnet"' \
        "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "claude-sonnet")
    log "  Provider[$STORY_PROVIDER] -> CLI=$(provider_to_cli "$STORY_PROVIDER")"
}

# provider_to_cli <provider>
# Returns the CLI binary name for a given aiProvider value.
provider_to_cli() {
    case "$1" in
        opencode)       echo "opencode" ;;
        codex)          echo "codex" ;;
        codemie-claude) echo "codemie-claude" ;;
        epam)           echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # epam: treat same as claude for now
        *)              echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # claude-sonnet, claude-opus, etc.
    esac
}

# normalize_provider_json <provider> <raw_jsonl_file> <out_json_file>
# Converts provider-specific JSONL output into a normalized JSON object
# matching Claude's format: {result, total_cost_usd, usage.{input_tokens,output_tokens}}
normalize_provider_json() {
    local provider="$1"
    local raw_file="$2"
    local out_file="$3"
    case "$provider" in
        opencode)
            # OpenCode emits JSONL stream; try step_finish first, then fall back to any cost/usage field
            local sf_line
            sf_line=$(grep '"type":"step_finish"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
            if [ -z "$sf_line" ]; then
                sf_line=$(grep -E '"cost"|"total_cost"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo '{}')
            fi
            sf_line="${sf_line:-{\}}"
            # Extract text parts for result summary
            local result_text
            result_text=$(grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
                | jq -rs '[.[].part.text // .[].text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "opencode run completed")
            jq -n \
                --arg rt "$result_text" \
                --argjson sf "$sf_line" \
                '{result: $rt,
                  total_cost_usd: ($sf.cost // $sf.part.cost // $sf.total_cost // 0),
                  usage: {
                      input_tokens:  ($sf.tokens.input  // $sf.part.tokens.input  // $sf.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
                      output_tokens: ($sf.tokens.output // $sf.part.tokens.output // $sf.usage.output_tokens // 0)
                  }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
            ;;
        codex)
            # Codex emits JSONL stream; turn.completed has usage (no cost field)
            local tc_line
            tc_line=$(grep '"type":"turn.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
            tc_line="${tc_line:-{\}}"
            local result_text
            result_text=$(grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
                | jq -rs '[.[].item.text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "codex exec completed")
            jq -n \
                --arg rt "$result_text" \
                --argjson tc "$tc_line" \
                '{result: $rt,
                  total_cost_usd: 0,
                  usage: {
                      input_tokens:  ($tc.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
                      output_tokens: ($tc.usage.output_tokens // 0)
                  }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
            ;;
        codemie-claude)
            # codemie-claude: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
            ;;
        epam)
            # epam: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
            ;;
        *)
            # Claude: already emits normalized JSON; nothing to do
            ;;
    esac
}

# Claude CLI permission flags
# These allow Claude to read/write files and execute commands without prompting
CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
    "--dangerously-skip-permissions"  # Skip all permission prompts for autonomous operation
)

# Alternative: Use granular permissions (uncomment if preferred over skip-permissions)
# CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
#     "--allowedTools" "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash"
# )

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
WHITE='\033[1;37m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Logging functions
log() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')]${NC} $1"
    echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
}

error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1" >&2
    echo "[ERROR] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
}

success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
    echo "[SUCCESS] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
}

warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
    echo "[WARNING] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
}

info() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

# Initialize directories and logs
initialize() {
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$PROGRESS_LOG")"
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR"

    if [ ! -f "$PROGRESS_LOG" ]; then
        cat > "$PROGRESS_LOG" << EOF
=== EPAM CLI Orchestration Progress Log ===
Started: $(date)
Project: $(jq -r '.project.name // "Unknown"' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
==========================================

EOF
    fi
}

# Check prerequisites
check_prerequisites() {
    # Check for jq
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        error "jq is required but not installed. Install with: sudo apt install jq"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for Claude CLI
    if ! command -v "$CLAUDE_CMD" &> /dev/null; then
        error "Claude CLI not found. Expected command: $CLAUDE_CMD"
        error "Install Claude Code CLI or set CLAUDE_CMD environment variable"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check PRD file
    if [ ! -f "$PRD_FILE" ]; then
        error "PRD file not found at $PRD_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Validate PRD JSON
    if ! jq empty "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        error "PRD file is not valid JSON"
        exit 1
    fi

    success "Prerequisites check passed"
}

# Get story details from PRD
get_story_details() {
    local story_id=$1
    jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id)' "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Get story title
get_story_title() {
    local story_id=$1
    jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title' "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Get story priority (high=1, medium=2, low=3)
get_story_priority() {
    local story_id=$1
    local priority=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .priority // "medium"' "$PRD_FILE")
    case $priority in
        high) echo 1 ;;
        medium) echo 2 ;;
        low) echo 3 ;;
        *) echo 2 ;;
    esac
}

# Check if story exists
story_exists() {
    local story_id=$1
    local exists=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .id' "$PRD_FILE")
    [ -n "$exists" ]
}

# Check if story is completed
is_story_completed() {
    local story_id=$1
    local completed=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .completed' "$PRD_FILE")
    [ "$completed" = "true" ]
}

# Get story dependencies
get_story_dependencies() {
    local story_id=$1
    jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .dependencies // [] | .[]' "$PRD_FILE"
}

# Check if all dependencies are satisfied (completed)
are_dependencies_satisfied() {
    local story_id=$1
    local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id")

    if [ -z "$deps" ]; then
        return 0  # No dependencies
    fi

    while IFS= read -r dep; do
        if [ -n "$dep" ] && ! is_story_completed "$dep"; then
            return 1  # Dependency not satisfied
        fi
    done <<< "$deps"

    return 0  # All dependencies satisfied
}

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# check_plan_mode_required <story_id>
# Returns 0 (true) when the story's complexity triggers plan mode.
# Triggers: estimatedHours >= 6, OR deps >= 2, OR planModeRequired flag.
# Bypass: SKIP_PLAN_MODE=true env var.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
check_plan_mode_required() {
    local story_id="$1"
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"

    [ "${SKIP_PLAN_MODE:-false}" = "true" ] && return 1

    local estimated_hours dep_count plan_flag
    estimated_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    dep_count=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | (.dependencies // []) | length' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    plan_flag=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .technicalNotes.planModeRequired // false' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo false)

    [ "$plan_flag" = "true" ] && return 0
    # Use awk for float comparison (avoids bc dependency for this check)
    awk -v h="$estimated_hours" 'BEGIN{exit !(h >= 6)}' && return 0
    [ "${dep_count:-0}" -ge 2 ] && return 0
    return 1
}

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# run_plan_mode <story_id>
# Invokes Claude in planning mode to produce execution-ready artifacts.
# Posts a plan_summary message to agent-messages.jsonl when complete.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
run_plan_mode() {
    local story_id="$1"
    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local plan_log="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_plan_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
    local plan_json="${plan_log%.log}_result.json"
    local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"

    local agent_role
    agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
        '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

    local plan_prompt
    plan_prompt=$(cat << PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
You are a planning agent. Produce an execution-ready plan for story ${story_id} BEFORE implementation begins.

## Required Outputs
1. Implementation steps with target file paths
2. Dependency validation (each dep: satisfied yes/no, reason)
3. Risk register (top 3 risks + mitigations)
4. Test plan (new tests required + regression scope)
5. Acceptance criteria mapping (each criterion -> implementation step)
6. Cost/effort forecast (confirm or adjust estimatedHours)

## On Completion
Append a single-line JSON record to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl:
{
  "id":"plan_${story_id}_\$(date +%s)",
  "timestamp":"\$(date -Iseconds)",
  "from_agent":"plan-agent",
  "to_agent":"${agent_role}",
  "story_id":"${story_id}",
  "phase_id":"${CURRENT_PHASE:-unknown}",
  "message_type":"plan_summary",
  "priority":"normal",
  "subject":"Plan ready for ${story_id}",
  "body":"<one-sentence summary of key risks/steps>",
  "status":"new"
}
Write it atomically: (flock -w 10 9 >> orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl; printf '%s\n' '<json>' >&9) 9>>orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl

## Story to Plan
Read orchestrations/prd.json for story ${story_id} full details, then produce the plan above.
PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
    )

    log "Plan mode: generating execution plan for $story_id..."
    touch "$messages_jsonl"
    cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
    if echo "$plan_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
            "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" 2>/dev/null > "$plan_json"; then
        jq -r '.result // empty' "$plan_json" 2>/dev/null >> "$plan_log" || true
        success "Plan mode completed for $story_id — see $plan_log"
    else
        warning "Plan mode failed for $story_id — continuing with direct implementation"
    fi
}

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# post_completion_message <story_id> <status>
# Appends a status message to agent-messages.jsonl after each story run.
# Only writes when ORCH_MODE=hybrid OR the bus file already exists.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
post_completion_message() {
    local story_id="$1"
    local status="$2"   # "completed" | "failed"
    local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"
    local lock_file="${messages_jsonl}.lock"

    # Skip if bash-only mode and bus hasn't been seeded yet
    if [ "${ORCH_MODE:-bash}" != "hybrid" ] && [ ! -f "$messages_jsonl" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
    local agent_role
